questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potential of SHE (Standard Hydrogen Electrode) is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. SHE potential = 0.00 V by definition. All other electrode potentials are measured relative to it.
questions.ncert_line: The standard hydrogen electrode (SHE) is the reference electrode and its potential is arbitrarily set to 0.00 V.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: During electrolysis of dilute H₂SO₄, the gas liberated at the anode is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Anode = oxidation → O₂ liberated. Cathode = reduction → H₂ liberated.
questions.ncert_line: At anode (oxidation): 2H2O → O2 + 4H+ + 4e−. Oxygen is liberated at anode during electrolysis of dilute H2SO4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following INCREASES the conductance of an electrolytic solution?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. More ions → higher conductance. Temperature increase also increases conductance (opposite of metals).
questions.ncert_line: Conductance increases with increase in ion concentration. More ions = more charge carriers = higher conductance.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to Faraday's first law of electrolysis, the mass of substance deposited is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. w ∝ Q (charge passed). w = ZQ = ZIt.
questions.ncert_line: Faraday's first law: mass deposited (w) ∝ quantity of electricity (Q = It). w = ZIt where Z is electrochemical equivalent.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molar conductivity of a strong electrolyte at infinite dilution can be determined by:

options.B: Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Kohlrausch's law: λ∞m = ν+λ∞+ + ν−λ∞−. Used for strong electrolytes at infinite dilution.
questions.ncert_line: For strong electrolytes, λ∞m cannot be measured directly (conductance still measurable at high dilution). Kohlrausch's law is used.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The rate of a reaction is independent of concentration of reactants. The order of this reaction is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Zero order — rate is constant, independent of concentration. Example: decomposition of NH₃ on Pt.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Zero order reaction: rate = k[A]0 = k. The rate is constant and independent of reactant concentration.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The half-life of a first-order reaction is 14 minutes. The time required for 99% completion is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. 99% completion ≈ 6.64 × t1/2 = 6.64 × 14 ≈ 93 min for first order reactions.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.3

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The Arrhenius equation is k = Ae^(−Ea/RT). Here 'A' represents:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. A = frequency factor (pre-exponential factor). Ea = activation energy, R = gas constant, T = temperature.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.5
questions.ncert_line: In Arrhenius equation, A is the frequency factor (pre-exponential factor) representing collision frequency.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a colligative property?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Osmotic pressure is a colligative property. It depends on mole fraction of solute, not its nature.
questions.ncert_line: Colligative properties depend on number of solute particles. Osmotic pressure, boiling point elevation, freezing point depression and vapour pressure lowering are colligative.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The Van't Hoff factor (i) for Na₂SO₄ which dissociates completely in water is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Na₂SO₄ gives 3 ions (2Na+ + SO42−), so i = 3.
questions.ncert_line: Na2SO4 → 2Na+ + SO42−. Total particles = 3. Van't Hoff factor i = 3 for complete dissociation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following pairs of liquids is IMMISCIBLE?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Water and benzene are immiscible — polar and non-polar liquids don't mix.
questions.ncert_line: Water (polar) and benzene (non-polar) are immiscible. Like dissolves like — polar dissolves polar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Henry's law states that the solubility of a gas in a liquid is:

options.B: Inversely proportional to partial pressure of gas
options.C: Directly proportional to partial pressure of gas

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Henry's law: solubility ∝ partial pressure of gas above solution.
questions.ncert_line: Henry's law: p = KH × x. Solubility (mole fraction x) = p/KH. Solubility is directly proportional to partial pressure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Lucas test is used to distinguish between:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Lucas test distinguishes 1°, 2°, 3° alcohols. 3° alcohol reacts immediately giving turbidity.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 11 — Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers, Section 11.3
questions.ncert_line: Lucas test (ZnCl2 + conc. HCl) distinguishes 1°, 2° and 3° alcohols. 3° reacts immediately, 2° in 5 min, 1° doesn't react at room temp.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT give a positive Tollens' test?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Acetone (ketone) gives NEGATIVE Tollens' test. All aldehydes give positive (silver mirror).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: Tollens' test (silver mirror test) is positive for aldehydes. Ketones like acetone do NOT reduce Tollens' reagent.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH3CHO is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. CH3CHO = Ethanal. Methanal = HCHO (formaldehyde). Propanal = CH3CH2CHO.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.1
questions.ncert_line: CH3CHO = acetaldehyde. IUPAC name: ethanal (2 carbons, aldehyde functional group -al).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which reagent converts an aldehyde to a primary alcohol?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. LiAlH4 reduces RCHO → RCH2OH (primary alcohol). Tollens'/Fehling's oxidise aldehydes.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: LiAlH4 (lithium aluminium hydride) reduces aldehydes to primary alcohols and ketones to secondary alcohols.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest base among amines?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Dimethylamine (2° amine) is strongest base in aqueous solution due to +I effect and solvation.
questions.ncert_line: In aqueous solution, basicity order of aliphatic amines: (CH3)2NH > CH3NH2 > (CH3)3N > NH3 > C6H5NH2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which gas is produced when concentrated H₂SO₄ reacts with copper?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. SO₂ is produced. Dilute H₂SO₄ does not react with Cu (below H in activity series).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.9
questions.ncert_line: Cu + 2H2SO4(conc.) → CuSO4 + SO2 + 2H2O. Hot concentrated H2SO4 is an oxidising acid — produces SO2 with Cu.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The hybridisation of sulphur in SF6 is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. SF6 = sp3d2 hybridisation. 6 S-F bonds, octahedral shape, bond angle 90°.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.9
questions.ncert_line: SF6: S has 6 bond pairs and 0 lone pairs → octahedral geometry → sp3d2 hybridisation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following oxides of nitrogen is an anhydride of nitric acid (HNO₃)?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. N2O5 is the acid anhydride of HNO₃. N2O3 is the anhydride of HNO₂.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.8
questions.ncert_line: N2O5 + H2O → 2HNO3. N2O5 is the anhydride of HNO3. N2O3 is the anhydride of HNO2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following transition metals has the highest melting point?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Tungsten has the highest melting point (3422°C) among all metals.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: Tungsten (W) has the highest melting point of all metals — 3422°C, due to strong metallic bonding from unpaired d electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: KMnO4 acts as an oxidising agent in acidic medium. Mn is reduced from +7 to:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. In acidic medium KMnO4 is reduced to Mn2+ (+7 → +2). In neutral/basic medium → MnO₂ (+4).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.4
questions.ncert_line: In acidic medium: MnO4− + 8H+ + 5e− → Mn2+ + 4H2O. Mn goes from +7 to +2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of [Co(NH₃)6]Cl3 is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Hexaamminecobalt(III) chloride. Ligands named first, metal with OS in Roman numerals, anion last.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.3
questions.ncert_line: IUPAC: name ligands alphabetically, then metal with oxidation state, then counter ion. [Co(NH3)6]Cl3 = hexaamminecobalt(III) chloride.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The geometry of [Ni(CN)4]2− is square planar because:

options.B: CN− is a strong field ligand causing d8 configuration to adopt square planar
options.D: Ni has completely filled d orbitals

questions.explanation: Correct: B. CN− strong field + d8 Ni2+ → square planar (dsp2). Weak field d8 → tetrahedral (sp3).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.5
questions.ncert_line: CN− is a strong field ligand. For d8 Ni2+, strong field causes pairing → square planar with dsp2 hybridisation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following complexes shows optical isomerism?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. [Co(en)3]3+ shows optical isomerism (Δ and Λ). It has no plane of symmetry.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.6
questions.ncert_line: [Co(en)3]3+ with three bidentate en ligands is chiral — it has non-superimposable mirror images (Δ and Λ forms).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The EAN (Effective Atomic Number) rule was given by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Sidgwick proposed EAN rule. Werner proposed coordination theory.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.1
questions.ncert_line: The EAN rule (effective atomic number rule) was proposed by N.V. Sidgwick. It states complexes are stable when metal attains noble gas configuration.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of radial nodes in 3s orbital is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Radial nodes = n−l−1 = 3−0−1 = 2 for 3s orbital.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Radial nodes = n − l − 1. For 3s: n=3, l=0. Radial nodes = 3−0−1 = 2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The shape of PCl5 molecule is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. PCl5 = trigonal bipyramidal, sp3d hybridisation. Bond angles 90° and 120°.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding, Section 4.5
questions.ncert_line: PCl5: P has 5 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → trigonal bipyramidal shape, sp3d hybridisation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest bond order?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. N₂ bond order = 3 (triple bond). Highest bond order = strongest bond.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding, Section 4.7
questions.ncert_line: Bond order: N2=3, O2=2, F2=1, Cl2=1. N2 has triple bond (highest bond order) and strongest, shortest bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a spontaneous reaction at constant T and P, the Gibbs free energy change (ΔG) must be:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ΔG < 0 for spontaneous process. ΔG = 0 at equilibrium. ΔG > 0 for non-spontaneous.
questions.ncert_line: ΔG < 0 for spontaneous reactions at constant T and P. ΔG = ΔH − TΔS.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction N₂ + 3H2 ⇌ 2NH3, if pressure is increased, the equilibrium shifts:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Increased pressure → equilibrium shifts to fewer moles of gas → towards NH₃ (products).
questions.ncert_line: Le Chatelier's principle: increasing pressure favours side with fewer moles of gas. Products (2 mol) < reactants (4 mol).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The pH of a 0.001 M HCl solution is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. [H+] = 10⁻³ M → pH = 3.
questions.ncert_line: HCl is a strong acid, fully dissociates. [H+] = 0.001 = 10⁻³ M. pH = −log[H+] = 3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following salts will give an acidic solution on dissolving in water?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. NH4Cl gives acidic solution. Salt of weak base + strong acid → acidic.
questions.ncert_line: NH4Cl = salt of weak base (NH3) + strong acid (HCl). NH4+ hydrolyses: NH4+ + H2O → NH3 + H3O+. pH < 7.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Which property increases regularly across a period from left to right?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Electronegativity increases left to right across a period. Atomic radius decreases.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.6
questions.ncert_line: Electronegativity increases across a period (left to right) as nuclear charge increases with constant shielding.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: The element with the highest electronegativity is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Fluorine = most electronegative element (3.98). Oxygen = 3.44, Nitrogen = 3.04.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.6
questions.ncert_line: Fluorine has the highest electronegativity (3.98 on Pauling scale) of all elements.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of radial nodes in a 3p orbital is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Radial nodes = n − l − 1 = 3−1 = 1. Angular nodes = l = 1 for p orbital.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Number of radial nodes = n − l − 1 = 3 − 1 − 1 = 1 for 3p orbital.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which quantum number determines the shape of an orbital?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. l determines shape. n determines size/energy, ml determines orientation, ms determines spin.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Azimuthal quantum number l determines orbital shape: l=0 (s, spherical), l=1 (p, dumbbell), l=2 (d, complex).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The geometry of NH₃ molecule is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. NH₃ = trigonal pyramidal (3 bp + 1 lp). BF3 = trigonal planar (3 bp + 0 lp).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding, Section 4.6
questions.ncert_line: NH3: sp3 hybridised, 3 bond pairs + 1 lone pair → trigonal pyramidal shape. Bond angle ~107°.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecules has the highest bond order?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. N₂ has triple bond — bond order 3 (highest). Higher bond order = shorter, stronger bond.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding, Section 4.7
questions.ncert_line: Bond order: N2 = 3 (triple bond), O2 = 2 (double bond), F2 = 1 (single bond), H2 = 1 (single bond).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a spontaneous process at constant T and P, the Gibbs free energy change (ΔG) must be:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Spontaneous: ΔG < 0. This is the criterion for spontaneity at constant T and P.
questions.ncert_line: Spontaneous process: ΔG < 0. ΔG = ΔH − TΔS. At equilibrium ΔG = 0. Non-spontaneous: ΔG > 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction N₂ + 3H2 ⇌ 2NH3, the equilibrium constant Kp is related to Kc by:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Δn = moles of gaseous products − moles of gaseous reactants = 2−4 = −2. Kp = Kc(RT)⁻².
questions.ncert_line: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn. Δn = 2 − (1+3) = −2. So Kp = Kc(RT)⁻².

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Le Chatelier's principle states that when a system at equilibrium is disturbed, it will:

options.A: Always shift to produce more products
options.C: Always shift to produce more reactants

questions.explanation: Correct: B. System shifts to counteract disturbance. Increase pressure → shift to fewer moles of gas.
questions.ncert_line: Le Chatelier's principle: system shifts to oppose the stress and restore equilibrium.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a buffer solution?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. NaCl + HCl is NOT a buffer. It's a strong acid + salt with no buffering action.
questions.ncert_line: Buffer = weak acid + its conjugate base OR weak base + its conjugate acid. NaCl + HCl = strong acid/salt, not a buffer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Electronegativity generally increases along a period because:

options.B: Nuclear charge increases and atomic radius decreases

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Electronegativity ∝ nuclear charge and ∝ 1/atomic radius. Both factors increase left to right.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.8
questions.ncert_line: Along a period: nuclear charge increases, atomic radius decreases → stronger pull on bonding electrons → higher electronegativity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: In SN2 reactions, the nucleophile attacks from:

options.B: Opposite side to the leaving group (backside attack)

questions.explanation: Correct: B. SN2 = backside attack → Walden inversion. Complete inversion of stereochemistry.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: SN2 mechanism: nucleophile attacks carbon from opposite side (180°) to leaving group — inversion of configuration.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Hyperconjugation involves delocalisation of electrons from:

options.A: π bond to adjacent p orbital
options.B: σ C-H bond to adjacent empty p orbital or π system
options.C: Lone pair to adjacent π bond

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Hyperconjugation = σ C-H electrons delocalised into adjacent π or empty p orbital. More C-H bonds = more stability.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Organic Chemistry: Basic Principles, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: Hyperconjugation: electrons from C-H σ bond delocalise into adjacent π system or empty p orbital.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Markovnikov's rule states that in addition of HX to an alkene, the H adds to the carbon with:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Markovnikov: H goes to carbon with more H (less substituted). This forms more stable carbocation.
questions.ncert_line: Markovnikov's rule: H adds to carbon with more H atoms (less substituted carbon). X adds to more substituted carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following undergoes electrophilic substitution rather than addition?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Benzene = electrophilic substitution (preserves aromaticity). Alkenes = electrophilic addition.
questions.ncert_line: Benzene undergoes electrophilic aromatic substitution (not addition) to preserve aromaticity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of CHCl3 is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. CHCl3 = trichloromethane. CHCl3 is chloroform (common name). CCl4 = tetrachloromethane.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.1
questions.ncert_line: CHCl3 = trichloromethane (IUPAC). Common name = chloroform. CH2Cl2 = dichloromethane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The secondary structure of proteins is maintained by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. H-bonds stabilise secondary structure (α-helix, β-pleated sheet).
questions.ncert_line: Secondary structure (alpha helix, beta sheet) is maintained by hydrogen bonds between -C=O and -N-H groups.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which vitamin deficiency causes scurvy?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Vitamin C deficiency → scurvy. Vitamin D deficiency → rickets. Vitamin A deficiency → night blindness.
questions.ncert_line: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) deficiency causes scurvy — characterised by bleeding gums and poor wound healing.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Nylon-6,6 is formed by condensation polymerisation of:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Nylon-6,6 = hexamethylene diamine + adipic acid. Nylon-6 = caprolactam only.
questions.ncert_line: Nylon-6,6: formed from hexamethylene diamine (6C) + adipic acid (6C) → polyamide with peptide-like linkages.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which allotrope of phosphorus is most reactive?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. White phosphorus most reactive — stored under water. Red phosphorus is stable and less reactive.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.7
questions.ncert_line: White phosphorus is the most reactive allotrope — it ignites spontaneously in air at 35°C (P4 molecules with high ring strain).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following oxoacids of sulphur contains an S-S bond?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. H2S2O3 (thiosulphuric acid) contains S-S bond. H2S2O7 has S-O-S linkage, no S-S bond.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.9
questions.ncert_line: Thiosulphuric acid H2S2O3 has an S-S bond (one S replaces O in sulphuric acid). Pyrosulphuric acid has S-O-S linkage.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following transition metals is liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Mercury (Hg) is liquid at room temperature. It is a transition metal (d-block element).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature (melting point −39°C).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The colour of transition metal complexes is due to:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. d-d transitions absorb specific wavelengths → complementary colour observed. Cu²⁺ = blue, Cr³⁺ = violet.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.5
questions.ncert_line: Transition metals absorb visible light for d-d transitions. The complementary colour is observed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The oxidation state of iron in [Fe(CN)6]³⁻ is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Fe + 6(−1) = −3 → Fe = +3. [Fe(CN)6]⁴⁻ has Fe²⁺. [Fe(CN)6]³⁻ has Fe³⁺.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.2
questions.ncert_line: Charge of complex = −3. CN⁻ has charge −1 × 6 = −6. Fe + (−6) = −3. Fe = +3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Adsorption of gases on solid surfaces is generally:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Adsorption is exothermic (ΔH < 0). As temperature increases, adsorption decreases.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 5 — Surface Chemistry, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: Adsorption is always exothermic — ΔH is negative. Entropy decreases so ΔS < 0. ΔG = ΔH − TΔS < 0 for spontaneous adsorption.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reverse osmosis is used in water purification. It involves applying pressure:

options.A: Less than osmotic pressure on pure water side
options.B: Greater than osmotic pressure on solution side
options.C: Equal to osmotic pressure on both sides
options.D: Less than osmotic pressure on solution side

questions.explanation: Correct: B. RO: pressure > π applied on solution side → water forced through semipermeable membrane.
questions.ncert_line: In RO, pressure greater than osmotic pressure is applied on solution side → solvent flows from solution to pure water side.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following sets of quantum numbers is NOT possible?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. l must be < n. n=2 allows l=0,1 only. l=2 requires n ≥ 3.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: For n=2, l can be 0 or 1 only (l < n). l=2 when n=2 is NOT allowed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard enthalpy of formation of an element in its standard state is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ΔfH° = 0 for all elements in standard state. e.g. ΔfH°(O₂, g) = 0, ΔfH°(C, graphite) = 0.
questions.ncert_line: By definition, ΔfH° of any element in its standard state = 0 (reference state).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The shape of XeF4 molecule is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. XeF4 = square planar. 2 lone pairs in axial positions cancel out → square planar molecular shape.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding, Section 4.6
questions.ncert_line: XeF4: sp3d2 hybridisation. 4 bond pairs + 2 lone pairs (octahedral electron geometry). Lone pairs at opposite axial positions → square planar shape.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Adsorption of gases on solids generally increases with:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. More surface area = more adsorption sites. Finely divided adsorbent > coarse material.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 5 — Surface Chemistry, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: Adsorption increases with surface area (more sites available) and decreases with temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a lyophilic colloid?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Starch = lyophilic (solvent-loving). Gold sol, AS2S3, sulphur sol = lyophobic.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 5 — Surface Chemistry, Section 5.4
questions.ncert_line: Lyophilic colloids: dispersed phase has affinity for dispersion medium. Starch, gelatin, gum = lyophilic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of atoms per unit cell in a face-centred cubic (FCC) structure is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. FCC = 4 atoms/unit cell. BCC = 2. Simple cubic = 1.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 1 — The Solid State, Section 1.3
questions.ncert_line: FCC: 8 corner atoms (×1/8) + 6 face atoms (×1/2) = 1 + 3 = 4 atoms per unit cell.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Schottky defect in ionic crystals causes:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Schottky defect decreases density (missing ions). Frenkel defect: no density change (ions displaced, not missing).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 1 — The Solid State, Section 1.5
questions.ncert_line: Schottky defect: equal number of cations and anions missing → fewer ions → lower density.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard EMF of a cell is related to Gibbs free energy by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. ΔG° = −nFE°. Also: ΔG° = −RT ln K. So E° = RT ln K / nF.
questions.ncert_line: ΔG° = −nFE°. Spontaneous cell reaction: E° > 0, so ΔG° < 0 (spontaneous). n = moles of electrons, F = Faraday.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Inductive effect is transmitted through:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Inductive effect through σ bonds, decreases with distance. Resonance through π bonds.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Organic Chemistry: Basic Principles, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Inductive effect is transmitted through σ bonds and decreases rapidly with distance from the functional group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The stability order of carbocations is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. 3° most stable carbocation. More alkyl groups provide electron density via hyperconjugation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Organic Chemistry: Basic Principles, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: Carbocation stability: 3° > 2° > 1° > methyl. More alkyl groups = more hyperconjugation and +I effect = more stable.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The combustion of alkanes is an example of:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Combustion = oxidation. Alkanes also undergo halogenation (free radical substitution).
questions.ncert_line: Complete combustion: CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O. Oxidation releases energy (exothermic).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which reagent is used to convert alkyl halide to alkane (reduction)?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Zn/HCl or LiAlH4 reduces R-X → R-H. Alcoholic KOH → alkene (elimination).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: Zn/HCl or LiAlH4 reduces R-X to R-H (alkane). Alcoholic KOH causes dehydrohalogenation (elimination).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following reactions is used to distinguish primary, secondary and tertiary amines?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Hinsberg test distinguishes 1°, 2°, 3° amines. Lucas test distinguishes 1°, 2°, 3° alcohols.
questions.ncert_line: Hinsberg test: benzenesulphonyl chloride reacts differently with 1°, 2°, 3° amines — used to distinguish them.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aniline reacts with acetic anhydride to give:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Aniline + acetic anhydride → acetanilide. This is acetylation (protection of amino group).
questions.ncert_line: Aniline + (CH3CO)2O → CH3CONHC6H5 (acetanilide) + CH3COOH. Acetylation of amine.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Conductivity of electrolytic solutions decreases on dilution. Reason (R): On dilution, ions move farther apart reducing inter-ionic attraction.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is false, R is true
options.D: A is false, R is false

questions.explanation: Correct: C. A is false — conductivity decreases on dilution but molar conductivity increases. R is true.
questions.ncert_line: On dilution: conductivity (κ) DECREASES (fewer ions/volume) but molar conductivity (Λm) INCREASES. So assertion A is FALSE.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Zero order t1/2 = [A]₀/2k ∝ [A]₀. First order t1/2 = 0.693/k (independent of concentration).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Zero-order: t1/2 = [A]₀/2k. Half-life is directly proportional to initial concentration. (Opposite of first order.)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Elevation in boiling point is a colligative property because it depends on:

options.C: Number of solute particles in solution

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Colligative = depends on number of particles only, not nature.
questions.ncert_line: Colligative properties depend only on the NUMBER of solute particles (moles), not on the nature or identity of solute.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following complexes has zero dipole moment?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. trans isomer: Cl-Pt-Cl and NH₃-Pt-NH₃ are linear — dipoles cancel. cis isomer has net dipole.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.6
questions.ncert_line: trans-[PtCl2(NH3)2]: identical ligands opposite each other → dipole moments cancel → zero net dipole moment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest oxidising agent among halogens?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. F₂ strongest oxidising agent. Oxidising power decreases down the group.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.13
questions.ncert_line: Oxidising power of halogens: F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2. F2 is strongest oxidising agent (highest E° = +2.87 V).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following transition metal ions is colourless?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Zn²+ (d¹⁰) is colourless. Colour in transition metal ions requires incomplete d subshell.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — d and f Block Elements, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Zn²+ has d¹⁰ configuration — completely filled d orbitals. No d-d transition possible → colourless.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements
questions.question: The first ionisation energy of Al is less than that of Mg because:

options.B: Al's 3p electron is easier to remove than Mg's 3s electron

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Al (3p¹) < Mg (3s²) ionisation energy. 3p electron more easily removed than 3s.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.7
questions.ncert_line: Al [Ne]3s²3p¹: the 3p electron has higher energy and is better shielded than Mg's 3s² electrons → easier to remove.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which alkali metal reacts most vigorously with water?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Cs reacts most vigorously. Reactivity increases down Group 1 (decreasing ionisation energy).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — s-Block Elements, Section 10.2
questions.ncert_line: Reactivity of alkali metals with water increases down the group: Li < Na < K < Rb < Cs. Caesium reacts explosively.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The anomalous behaviour of lithium compared to other alkali metals is due to:

options.B: Its small size and high charge density (diagonal relationship with Mg)

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Li resembles Mg (diagonal relationship) due to similar charge/size ratio.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — s-Block Elements, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: Li has smallest ionic radius and highest charge density among alkali metals → diagonal relationship with Mg.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Formic acid (HCOOH) is stronger than acetic acid (CH3COOH) because:

options.A: Formic acid has lower molecular weight
options.B: CH₃ group is electron-donating (+I effect) destabilising formate's conjugate base
options.C: Formic acid has more acidic H atoms
options.D: Formic acid is more soluble in water

questions.explanation: Correct: B. CH₃ (+I effect) destabilises acetate ion → acetic acid weaker. HCOOH pKa=3.74; CH3COOH pKa=4.76.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: CH3 group in acetic acid has +I effect → increases electron density → destabilises conjugate base (CH3COO-) → weaker acid than HCOOH.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) is formed by the reaction of salicylic acid with:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Aspirin = acetylation of salicylic acid using acetic anhydride. Industrial process.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.6
questions.ncert_line: Aspirin synthesis: salicylic acid + acetic anhydride (or acetyl chloride) → acetylsalicylic acid + acetic acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Bakelite is formed by condensation of phenol with:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Bakelite = phenol + formaldehyde. Cross-linked, thermosetting, does not remelt.
questions.ncert_line: Bakelite = phenol-formaldehyde resin. Cross-linked thermosetting polymer. First synthetic plastic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following salts gives an alkaline solution when dissolved in water?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Sodium acetate → alkaline solution. Salt of weak acid + strong base always gives alkaline solution.
questions.ncert_line: CH3COONa = salt of strong base (NaOH) + weak acid (CH3COOH). CH3COO- hydrolyses → CH3COOH + OH- → alkaline.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a first order reaction, a plot of log[A] vs time gives a straight line with slope:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Slope of log[A] vs t = −k/2.303 for first order reaction.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.3
questions.ncert_line: Integrated first order: log[A] = log[A]₀ − kt/2.303. Slope = −k/2.303.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest oxidising halogen?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. F₂ is the strongest oxidising agent. It can oxidise even water: 2F2 + 2H2O → 4HF + O₂.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.11
questions.ncert_line: Oxidising power of halogens: F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2. F2 is the strongest oxidising agent among all elements.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following oxoacids of chlorine has the highest oxidising power?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. HClO has highest oxidising power among oxoacids of Cl. HClO₄ is the strongest acid but weakest oxidiser.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.11
questions.ncert_line: Oxidising power of oxoacids decreases as the number of oxygen atoms increases. HClO (Cl in +1) is strongest oxidiser.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest first ionisation energy?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Mg > Al (exception). Full s² subshell in Mg is extra stable. Similarly N > O in same period.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.7
questions.ncert_line: IE generally increases across period, but Mg has higher IE1 than Al. Mg (full 3s²) is more stable than Al (3s²3p¹).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The colour of transition metal compounds is due to:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. d-d transitions absorb specific visible wavelengths → complementary colour observed. Cu²⁺ = blue (absorbs red).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Partially filled d orbitals allow d-d electronic transitions. Energy difference corresponds to visible light → coloured compounds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) is classified as:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Aspirin = analgesic + antipyretic. Also has anticoagulant properties (thins blood).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 16 — Chemistry in Everyday Life, Section 16.1
questions.ncert_line: Aspirin is an analgesic (pain-killer), antipyretic (fever-reducer) and anti-inflammatory drug.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A first-order reaction is 75% complete in 32 minutes. Its half-life is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. 75% done = 2 half-lives. 32 min ÷ 2 = 16 min half-life.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.3
questions.ncert_line: 75% complete means two half-lives (50% after 1st, 75% after 2nd). 2 half-lives = 32 min → t½ = 16 min.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Which of the following has the smallest atomic radius?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Cl has smallest radius. Order: Na > Mg > Al > Si > P > S > Cl (left to right).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.6
questions.ncert_line: Atomic radius decreases across a period (increasing nuclear charge). Cl (period 3, group 17) has smallest radius among these.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The oxidation state of Fe in K3[Fe(CN)6] is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. K3[Fe(CN)6]: Fe = +3. K4[Fe(CN)6] has Fe = +2. (Prussian blue involves both.)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.2

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following complexes does NOT show geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. [Co(en)3]3+ has no geometrical isomers (all ligands equivalent). It has optical isomers (Δ/Λ).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.6
questions.ncert_line: [Co(en)3]3+ has three identical bidentate ligands — no cis/trans isomerism possible. It only shows optical isomerism.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Carboxylic acids are stronger acids than alcohols because:

options.B: Carboxylate ion is stabilised by resonance
options.C: They are more soluble in water

questions.explanation: Correct: B. RCOO⁻ stabilised by resonance → easier to lose H+ → stronger acid. pKa ~4-5 vs ~16 for alcohols.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: Carboxylate ion (RCOO⁻) has resonance stabilisation — negative charge delocalised over both oxygens. Alkoxide (RO⁻) has no such stabilisation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aldol condensation requires an aldehyde or ketone with:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. α-hydrogen essential for aldol condensation. Benzaldehyde (no α-H) undergoes Cannizzaro reaction instead.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Aldol condensation requires α-hydrogen (H on carbon adjacent to carbonyl). Enolate ion attacks another carbonyl.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Bakelite is formed by condensation polymerisation between:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Bakelite = phenol + formaldehyde. Thermosetting, cross-linked. Used in electrical switches.
questions.ncert_line: Bakelite: phenol + formaldehyde → cross-linked polymer. First synthetic plastic. Thermosetting polymer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following transition metals does NOT show variable oxidation states?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Zn = only +2 (d¹⁰ fully filled). Fe = +2,+3. Cu = +1,+2. Mn = +2 to +7.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: Zinc has fully filled d orbitals (3d¹⁰) and shows only +2 oxidation state. No variable oxidation states like other transition metals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following ions is colourless?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Zn²⁺ (d¹⁰) is colourless. d-d transitions need partially filled d orbitals for colour.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Zn²⁺ has electronic configuration [Ar]3d¹⁰ — fully filled d orbitals, no d-d transitions possible → colourless.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The magnetic moment of a transition metal ion with 3 unpaired electrons is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. μ = √(n+2)) = √15 ≈ 3.87 BM for 3 unpaired electrons.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: μ = √n(n+2) BM where n = number of unpaired electrons. n=3: μ = √(3×5) = √15 ≈ 3.87 BM.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the complex [Fe(CN)6]³⁻, the oxidation state of Fe is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Fe + 6(−1) = −3 → Fe = +3. Compare [Fe(CN)6]⁴⁻ where Fe = +2.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.2
questions.ncert_line: CN⁻ is −1 each. Total charge of complex = −3. Fe + 6(−1) = −3. Fe = −3 + 6 = +3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following complexes will show geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Square planar MA2B2 → cis and trans isomers. cis-platin is the cis isomer (cancer drug).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.6
questions.ncert_line: [Pt(NH3)2Cl2] is square planar with two different ligands — shows cis (same side) and trans (opposite) isomers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reverse osmosis involves applying pressure:

options.A: Less than osmotic pressure on solvent side
options.B: Greater than osmotic pressure on solution side

questions.explanation: Correct: B. RO applies pressure > osmotic pressure on solution side → pure water passes to solvent side. Used in water purification.
questions.ncert_line: RO: external pressure greater than osmotic pressure applied on solution side forces pure solvent through semi-permeable membrane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following processes has ΔS > 0?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Sublimation increases entropy greatly (solid→gas). All phase changes to more disordered state have ΔS > 0.
questions.ncert_line: Sublimation: solid → gas. Huge increase in disorder (entropy). ΔS >> 0. All others involve decrease in randomness.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Buffer solutions resist change in pH because they contain:

options.A: A strong acid and its salt
options.B: A weak acid and its conjugate base (or weak base and its conjugate acid)
options.C: Equal concentrations of acid and base
options.D: A strong base and its salt

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Buffer contains weak acid + conjugate base. Henderson-Hasselbalch: pH = pKa + log([A⁻]/[HA]).
questions.ncert_line: Buffer = weak acid + conjugate base (or weak base + conjugate acid). Added H⁺ reacts with conjugate base; added OH⁻ reacts with weak acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following polymers is a natural polymer?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Cellulose = natural polymer (from plants). Starch, rubber, proteins are also natural polymers.
questions.ncert_line: Cellulose is a natural polymer of glucose (β-1,4 linkage). Nylon, Bakelite, PVC are all synthetic polymers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Williamson synthesis is used to prepare:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Williamson synthesis → ethers. Best method for unsymmetrical ethers.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 11 — Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers, Section 11.5
questions.ncert_line: Williamson synthesis: alkoxide + alkyl halide → ether. R-O⁻Na⁺ + R'X → R-O-R' + NaX.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aldol condensation requires:

options.A: An aldehyde or ketone with alpha-hydrogen and base catalyst

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Needs α-H and base. Formaldehyde (no α-H) undergoes Cannizzaro reaction instead.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Aldol condensation: carbonyl compound with at least one α-hydrogen + dilute base → β-hydroxy carbonyl compound.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following undergoes Cannizzaro reaction?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. HCHO has no α-H → Cannizzaro reaction (disproportionation). 2HCHO → HCOOH + CH3OH.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Cannizzaro reaction occurs with aldehydes lacking α-H (HCHO, C6H5CHO). Disproportionation gives acid + alcohol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Carbylamine reaction (isocyanide test) is specific for:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Carbylamine test (isocyanide formation) is specific for primary amines only.
questions.ncert_line: Carbylamine reaction: primary amine + CHCl3 + KOH → isocyanide (foul smell). Specific test for primary amines.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Nucleophilic substitution in haloarenes is much more difficult than in haloalkanes because:

options.A: C-X bond in arene is longer
options.B: C-X bond has partial double bond character due to resonance making it stronger
options.D: Halogen atoms in arenes are more electronegative

questions.explanation: Correct: B. C-X bond in arene is strengthened by resonance (partial double bond character) → less reactive to nucleophiles.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: In haloarenes, lone pair of halogen delocalises into benzene ring → C-X has partial double bond character → stronger → harder to cleave.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The anomeric carbon in glucose is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. C-1 is anomeric carbon. α-D-glucose has OH below ring; β-D-glucose has OH above ring at C-1.
questions.ncert_line: Anomeric carbon = C-1 in glucose (the carbonyl carbon in open chain form). Alpha and beta anomers differ at C-1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The enzyme that catalyses the hydrolysis of peptide bonds is classified as:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Proteases = hydrolases. Peptide bond hydrolysis: R-CO-NH-R' + H₂O → R-COOH + H2N-R'.
questions.ncert_line: Proteases (peptidases) are hydrolases that catalyse hydrolysis of peptide bonds (C-N bonds) in proteins.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the molar conductivity (Λm) of a 0.050 mol L⁻¹ solution of a monobasic weak acid is 90 S cm² mol⁻¹, its extent (degree) of dissociation will be [Λ⁰₊=349.6, Λ⁰₋=50.4 S cm² mol⁻¹]:
questions.explanation: Official NEET 2025 answer

questions.ncert_line: To be added — use this question as pattern template

questions.question: If the rate constant of a reaction is 0.03 s⁻¹, how much time does it take for 7.2 mol L⁻¹ concentration of the reactant to get reduced to 0.9 mol L⁻¹? (log 2=0.301)
questions.explanation: Official NEET 2025 answer

questions.question: The standard heat of formation, in kcal/mol of Ba²⁺ is [Standard heat of formation of SO₄²⁻(aq)=-216 kcal/mol, standard heat of crystallisation of BaSO₄(s)=-4.5 kcal/mol, standard heat of formation of BaSO₄(s)=-349 kcal/mol]:
questions.explanation: Official NEET 2025 answer
questions.ncert_line: To be added — use this question as pattern template

questions.question: Given below are two statements: Statement I: Ferromagnetism is considered as an extreme form of paramagnetism. Statement II: The number of unpaired electrons in a Cr²⁺ ion (Z=24) is the same as that of a Nd³⁺ ion (Z=60).

options.A: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
options.B: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
options.C: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
options.D: Statement I is true but Statement II is false

questions.explanation: Official NEET 2025 answer

questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Which of the following statements are true? A.Unlike Ga that has a very high melting point, Cs has a very low melting point. B.On Pauling scale, the electronegativity values of N and Cl are not the same. C.Ar, K⁺, Cl⁻, Ca²⁺, and S²⁻ are all isoelectronic species. D.The correct order of first ionization enthalpies of Na, Mg, Al, Si is Si>Al>Mg>Na. E.The atomic radius of Cs is greater than that of Li and Rb.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction A(g)⇌2B(g), the backward reaction rate constant is higher than the forward reaction rate constant by a factor of 2500, at 1000 K. Kp for the reaction at 1000 K is [R=0.0831 L atm mol⁻¹K⁻¹]:
questions.explanation: Official NEET 2025 answer

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a first order reaction, the half-life (t₁/₂) is:

options.D: Proportional to the square of initial concentration

questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2: First Order Reactions
questions.ncert_line: For a first order reaction, the integrated rate equation is: k = (2.303/t) log ([R]₀/[R]). The half-life of a first order reaction is: t₁/₂ = 0.693/k. It is independent of initial concentration.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molar conductivity (Λm) of a 0.050 mol L⁻¹ solution of a monobasic weak acid is 90 S cm² mol⁻¹ and Λ°m is 400 S cm² mol⁻¹. What is the degree of dissociation?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4: Conductance of Electrolytic Solutions
questions.ncert_line: The degree of dissociation (α) of a weak electrolyte is equal to the ratio of its molar conductivity at a given concentration (Λm) to its limiting molar conductivity (Λ°m): α = Λm / Λ°m.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a galvanic cell?

options.B: The anode is the negative terminal
options.D: The anode is the positive terminal

questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.1: Standard Electrode Potential
questions.ncert_line: In a galvanic (voltaic) cell, the electrode where oxidation occurs is the anode (negative terminal) and the electrode where reduction occurs is the cathode (positive terminal). The cell EMF = E°cathode − E°anode.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about order and molecularity is INCORRECT?

options.A: Order of a reaction can be zero or fractional
options.B: Molecularity is always a whole number
options.D: Molecularity can be zero for unimolecular reactions

questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.1.2: Order and Molecularity
questions.ncert_line: Order of a reaction is determined experimentally and can be zero, fractional or integer. Molecularity is the number of reacting species in an elementary reaction and is always a whole number. Molecularity cannot be zero or fractional.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life (t₁/₂):

options.B: Is directly proportional to initial concentration
options.C: Is inversely proportional to initial concentration

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.1: Zero Order Reactions
questions.ncert_line: For a zero order reaction, the rate is independent of concentration: rate = k. The integrated equation is [R] = [R]₀ − kt. The half-life is t₁/₂ = [R]₀/2k — it depends on initial concentration.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to the Arrhenius equation, a plot of log k vs 1/T gives a straight line. The slope of this line is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.4: Temperature Dependence
questions.ncert_line: The Arrhenius equation k = Ae^(−Ea/RT) shows that rate constant increases with temperature. The pre-exponential factor A (frequency factor) accounts for collision frequency. A plot of ln k vs 1/T gives slope = −Ea/R.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A solution shows negative deviation from Raoult's law. Which of the following is NOT true for such a solution?

options.A: The vapour pressure of the solution is lower than predicted by Raoult's law
options.C: The interactions between unlike molecules are stronger than like-molecule interactions

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.4: Vapour Pressure of Liquid Solutions
questions.ncert_line: Raoult's law: For a solution of volatile liquids, the partial vapour pressure of each component is directly proportional to its mole fraction: pA = x_A × p°A. An ideal solution obeys Raoult's law at all compositions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction N₂(g) + O₂(g) ⇌ 2NO(g), ΔH = +180.7 kJ mol⁻¹. Higher yield of NO is obtained by:

options.B: Higher temperature; pressure has no effect (Δn = 0)

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.7: Le Chatelier's Principle
questions.ncert_line: Le Chatelier's principle: If a system at equilibrium is disturbed by a change in concentration, temperature or pressure, the equilibrium will shift in the direction that tends to counteract the disturbance.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to Markovnikov's rule, when HBr adds to propene (CH₃-CH=CH₂), the bromine attaches to:

options.A: C₁ (terminal carbon with more H atoms)
options.D: The π bond breaks and Br adds to both carbons

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.2: Addition Reactions of Alkenes
questions.ncert_line: Markovnikov's rule: In the addition of HX to an unsymmetrical alkene, the hydrogen attaches to the carbon with more hydrogen atoms (the less substituted carbon) and the halide attaches to the more substituted carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Benzene has 6 π electrons. According to Hückel's rule (4n+2), this corresponds to n =
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.4.2: Aromaticity
questions.ncert_line: For aromaticity, a compound must be: (1) cyclic, (2) planar, (3) fully conjugated (each atom contributes a p-orbital), and (4) contain 4n+2 π electrons (Hückel's rule, n = 0, 1, 2...). Benzene (6 π electrons, n=1) is the classic example.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the Hofmann bromamide degradation (RCONH₂ + Br₂/NaOH → RNH₂), the product amine has:

options.A: The same number of carbon atoms as the amide
options.B: One more carbon atom than the amide
options.C: One less carbon atom than the amide
options.D: Two less carbon atoms than the amide

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Amines, Section 13.2.3: Hofmann Bromamide Degradation
questions.ncert_line: Hofmann bromamide degradation: When an amide (RCONH2) is treated with bromine in the presence of NaOH, it gives a primary amine with one carbon less (RNH2). This is a rearrangement reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The magnetic moment formula μ = √(n+2)) BM is used to determine the number of:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.7.3: Colour and Magnetic Properties
questions.ncert_line: The magnetic moment μ = √(n+2)) BM, where n is the number of unpaired electrons. Paramagnetic substances have unpaired electrons; diamagnetic substances have all electrons paired. Complexes with more unpaired electrons are more paramagnetic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of unpaired electrons in Cr²⁺ (Z = 24) is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d- and f-Block Elements, Section 8.2: Properties of Transition Metals

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about activation energy is INCORRECT?

options.B: Higher activation energy means slower reaction
options.D: It is the minimum energy required for reaction

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Activation energy is ALWAYS positive (energy barrier). It cannot be negative.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.5
questions.ncert_line: Activation energy (Ea) is always positive — it is the minimum energy barrier that must be overcome for reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction 2A → B, the rate of disappearance of A is 0.04 mol/L/s. The rate of formation of B is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Rate of B = 0.02 mol/L/s. Since 2 moles A give 1 mole B, rate of B = half rate of A disappearance.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.1
questions.ncert_line: Rate = −(1/2)d[A]/dt = d[B]/dt. Rate of B formation = (1/2) × rate of A disappearance = 0.04/2 = 0.02 mol/L/s.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A reaction has ΔH = −100 kJ and ΔS = −200 J/K. At what temperature will the reaction become non-spontaneous?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Non-spontaneous above 500 K. At T = 500K: ΔG = 0 (equilibrium). Above 500K: ΔG > 0 (non-spontaneous).
questions.ncert_line: ΔG = ΔH − TΔS = −1000 − T(−200) = −1000 + 200T. Non-spontaneous when ΔG > 0: 200T > 1000 → T > 500 K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction at 300K: ΔH = −57.1 kJ/mol and ΔS = −80 J/mol/K. The reaction is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ΔG = −57100 + 24000 = −33100 J < 0 → spontaneous at 300K.

questions.ncert_line: Moles = mass/molecular weight = 44/44 = 1 mol.

questions.question: In the reaction 2H2 + O₂ → 2H2O, the mass of water produced from 4g of H₂ is:

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 1 — Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry, Section 1.6
questions.ncert_line: 4g H2 = 2 mol H2. From equation: 2 mol H2 → 2 mol H2O. 2 mol H2O = 2 × 18 = 36 g.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The mass deposited at cathode when 2 Faradays of charge is passed through CuSO4 solution is: (Cu = 64 g/mol)
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cu²⁺ needs 2F to deposit 1 mol (64g). 2F → 64g Cu.
questions.ncert_line: Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu. 2 Faradays = 2 mol e⁻. From equation: 2 mol e⁻ deposits 1 mol Cu = 64 g.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction at 298 K, ΔH = −100 kJ/mol and ΔS = −200 J/mol·K. The reaction is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ΔH < 0, ΔS < 0 → ΔG = ΔH − TΔS. Spontaneous (ΔG < 0) only at low T (below 500 K).
questions.ncert_line: ΔG = ΔH − TΔS = −1000 − T(−200) = −1000 + 200T. ΔG < 0 when T < 500 K. Spontaneous at low T.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: At equilibrium, for the reaction N₂ + O₂ ⇌ 2NO, if Kc = 4 × 10⁻³¹ at 298 K, this means:

options.B: Reaction is reactant-favoured (very little NO at equilibrium)
options.C: Equal amounts of reactants and products

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Kc << 1 → reactant-favoured. Almost no NO formed at room temperature.
questions.ncert_line: Very small Kc (4×10⁻³¹) means equilibrium lies far to the left — very little NO at equilibrium. Reaction barely proceeds.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.ncert_line: moles glucose = 18/180 = 0.1 mol. Molality = 0.1/0.1 = 1 mol/kg. ΔTb = Kb × m = 0.52 × 1 = 0.52 K.

options.A: (a) and (b) are elimination reaction and (c) is addition reaction
options.B: (a) is elimination, (b) is substitution and (c) is addition reaction
options.C: (a) is elimination, (b) and (c) are substitution reactions
options.D: (a) is substitution, (b) and (c) are addition reaction

questions.explanation: (a) CH3CH2Br+KOH→CH₃CH=CH₂+KBr+H2OCH3CH2Br+KOH→CH₃CH=CH₂+KBr+H₂O breaking of 2 bonds and formation of 1 bond so it is an example of elimination reaction. (Image b) replacement of Br− by OH− is substitution reaction (Image c) breaking of 1 bond and formation of 2 bonds is addition reaction

questions.explanation: Stability ∝ bond order. Bond orders: O₂⁺ = 2.5, O₂ = 2.0, O₂⁻ = 1.5, O₂²⁻ = 1.0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of water molecules is maximum in
questions.explanation: 18 moles of water = 18 × Nₐ molecules of H₂O, which is the largest among all options.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following pairs, both the species are not isostructural?
questions.explanation: XeF₄ is square planar (2 lone pairs) while XeO₄ is tetrahedral (0 lone pairs) — they are not isostructural.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the reaction with HCl, an alkene reacts in accordance with the Markovnikov's rule, to give a product 1-chloro-1-methylcyclohexane. The possible alkene is
questions.explanation: Both methylenecyclohexane and 1-methylcyclohexene react with HCl via Markovnikov's rule to give 1-chloro-1-methylcyclohexane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assuming complete ionization, same moles of which of the following compounds will require the least amount of acidified KMnO₄ for complete oxidation?
questions.explanation: MnO₄⁻ → Mn²⁺; Change in O.N. = 5. FeSO₄ gives only Fe²⁺ → Fe³⁺ change of 1 electron total, requiring least KMnO₄.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reaction of phenol with chloroform in presence of dilute sodium hydroxide finally introduces which one of the following functional group?
questions.explanation: The Reimer-Tiemann reaction: phenol + CHCl₃ + NaOH → ortho-hydroxybenzaldehyde (–CHO group introduced).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The vacant space in bcc lattice unit cell is
questions.explanation: Packing fraction in bcc lattice is 68%. Therefore the vacant space = 32%.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the statements given below is incorrect?

options.B: OF₂ is an oxide of fluorine
options.C: Cl₂O₇ is an anhydride of perchloric acid

questions.explanation: In oxides, oxidation state of oxygen is –2, but in OF₂, oxidation state of oxygen is +2 because F is more electronegative than oxygen. So OF₂ is a fluoride of oxygen, not an oxide of fluorine.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The name of complex ion, [Fe(CN)₆]³⁻ is
questions.explanation: [Fe(CN)₆]³⁻: Hexacyanidoferrate(III) ion.

options.B: The ratio of elements to each other in a compound
options.C: The definition of mass in units of grams
options.D: The mass of one mole of carbon

questions.question: Which of the following statements is not correct for a nucleophile?

options.A: Nucleophiles attack low e⁻ density sites

questions.explanation: Nucleophiles are electron rich species having ability to donate lone pair of electrons and thus they are Lewis bases, not Lewis acids.

questions.explanation: On increasing temperature and decreasing pressure, gases tend to behave ideally.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The heat of combustion of carbon to CO₂ is –393.5 kJ/mol. The heat released upon formation of 35.2 g of CO₂ from carbon and oxygen gas is
questions.explanation: For 44 g CO₂ heat released = –393.5 kJ. For 35.2 g CO₂: –(393.5/44) × 35.2 = –314.8 kJ ≈ –315 kJ.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: What is the mole fraction of the solute in a 1.00 m aqueous solution?
questions.explanation: Mole fraction of solute = 1/(55.5 + 1) = 1/56.5 = 0.0177.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The stability of +1 oxidation state among Al, Ga, In and Tl increases in the sequence
questions.explanation: Due to inert pair effect, group oxidation state less by 2 becomes more stable going down the group. Group oxidation state of group 13 is +3. Stability of +1 state: Al < Ga < In < Tl.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: The synthesis of amides from amines with acyl halides or anhydrides in the presence of aqueous base is known as the Schotten-Baumann reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The sum of coordination number and oxidation number of the metal M in the complex [M(en)₂(C₂O₄)]Cl (where en is ethylenediamine) is
questions.explanation: Coordination number of M is 6 (2 en bidentate + 1 oxalate bidentate). Oxidation state of M is +3. Sum = 6 + 3 = 9.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reaction of a carbonyl compound with one of the following reagents involves nucleophilic addition followed by elimination of water. The reagent is

questions.explanation: C=O + H₂N–NH₂ (feebly acidic) → C=N–NH₂ + H₂O. Hydrazine undergoes nucleophilic addition followed by –H₂O elimination to form hydrazone.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: Since –NO₂ has strong –I and –R effect, it will favour hydrolysis of ester most.

questions.explanation: Order is zero (unit of rate constant is mol s⁻¹). [A]₀ – [A] = kt = 0.6 × 10⁻³ × 20 × 60 = 0.72 M. [B] = 0.72 M.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: What is the pH of the resulting solution when equal volumes of 0.1 M NaOH and 0.01 M HCl are mixed?
questions.explanation: Moles NaOH = 0.1V; Moles HCl = 0.01V. Excess NaOH = 0.09V in 2V total. [OH⁻] = 0.045 M. pOH = 1.35. pH = 12.65.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following reaction(s) can be used for the preparation of alkyl halides? (I) CH₃CH₂OH + HCl → (with anhydrous ZnCl₂) (II) CH₃CH₂OH + HCl → (III) (CH₃)₃COH + HCl → (IV) (CH₃)₂CHOH + HCl → (with anhydrous ZnCl₂)
questions.explanation: The reactions of primary and secondary alcohols with HCl require the presence of a catalyst ZnCl₂. Tertiary alcohols react without catalyst.

questions.question: Following limiting molar conductivities are given as λm∘(H₂SO₄)=x S cm2mol−1 λm∘(K2SO4)=y S cm2mol−1 λm∘(CH3COOK)=z S cm2mol−1 λm∘(in S cm2mol−1) for CH3COOH will be
questions.explanation: According to Kohlrausch's law λm∘(AB)=λm∘(A+)+λm∘(B−) λ°m(CH3COOH)=λ°m(CH3COO−)+λ°m(H+) So λ°m(CH3COOH) =λ°m(CH3COOK)+21λm0(H₂SO₄)−21λm0(K2SO4) =z+2x−2y =z+(2x−y)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction, activation energy Ea=0 and the rate constant at 200 K is 1.6×106s−1. The rate constant at 400 K will be [Given that gas constant, R=8.314J K−1mol−1]
questions.explanation: From Arrhenius equation logk200k400=2.303 REa[T1T2−T1] Since, given that Ea=0 So, logk200k400=0⇒k200k400=1 k400=k200 So rate constant at 400 k=1.6×106s−1

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct option representing a Freundlich adsorption isotherm is
questions.explanation: According to Freundlich isotherm mx=KP1/n (n>1) So, mx=KP0.3 as n1=0.3 So, Answer is (2).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A first order reaction has a rate constant 2.303×10−3s−1. The time required for 40 g of this reactant to reduce to 10 g will be [Given that log102=0.3010]
questions.explanation: For a first order reaction Half life period, t21=k0.693=2.303×10−30.693s−1 =300.91 s Now, 40 gt1/220 gt1/210 g So, 40 g substance requires 2 half life periods to reduce upto 10 g ... Time taken in reduction =2×300.91 s =601.82 ≈602 s

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is paramagnetic?
questions.explanation: O₂ has 8+8=16 electrons, so distribution of electrons in MO(molecular orbitals) follows the order as (σ₁ₛ)2,(σ*₁ₛ)2,(σ₂ₛ)2,(σ*₂ₛ)2,(σ₂ₚz)2(π₂ₚₓ)2(π*₂ₚₓ)1 (π₂ₚᵧ)2(π*₂ₚᵧ)1 So, in O₂ molecule, there are two (2) unpaired electrons, so, it is a "paramagnetic" substance in nature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the correct order of dipole moment?
questions.explanation: Dipole moment of a molecule is the vector sum of dipoles of bonds. So based on molecular geometry of following molecules, [Image showing vectors for BF3, NF3, NH₃, H₂O] Three equal vectors at 120∘ has resultant = 0 so nonpolar molecule Vectors not alligned in the same direction of lone pair so less dipole moment Vectors alligned in same direction of lone pair Maximum dipole moment

questions.explanation: Let, volume of solution = 1 litre Mole of NaOH=2 Mass of NaOH solution =1000×1.28=1280 g Mass of H₂O=(1280−2×40)=1200 g=1.2 kg Molality ( (m)=Mass of solvent(kg)Mole of NaOH =1.22 =1.67 m

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct regarding a solution of two components A and B exhibiting positive deviation from ideal behaviour?

options.A: Intermolecular attractive forces between A-A and B-B are equal to those between A-B.
options.B: Intermolecular attractive forces between A-A and B-B are stronger than those between A-B.
options.C: ΔmixH=0 at constant T and P.
options.D: ΔmixV=0 at constant T and P.

questions.explanation: Solution exhibiting positive deviation from ideal behaviour must have weaker A - B interactions than A - A and B - B interactions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In water saturated air, the mole fraction of water vapour is 0.02. If the total pressure of the saturated air is 1.2 atm, the partial pressure of dry air is

options.B: They are formed from bi-and tri-functional monomers.
options.C: They contain covalent bonds between various linear polymer chains.
options.D: They contain strong covalents bonds in their polymer chains.

Cross linked or network polymers are formed from bi- functional and tri- functional monomers and contain strong covalent bonds between various linear polymer chains, e.g. bakelite, melamine etc. Option (4) is not related to cross- linking. questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A mixture of 2.3 g formic acid and 4.5 g oxalic acid is treated with conc. H₂SO₄. The evolved gaseous mixture is passed through KOH pellets. Weight (in g) of the remaing product at STP will be
questions.explanation: Sol. HCOOH --Conc.H₂SO₄--> CO(g) + H₂O(l) (2.3g or 1/20 mol) COOH | --Conc.H₂SO₄--> CO(g) + CO₂(g) + H₂O(l) COOH (4.5g or 1/20 mol) (1/20 mol) (1/20 mol) Gaseous mixture formed is CO and CO₂ when it is passed through KOH, only CO₂ is absorbed. So the remaing gas is CO. So, weight of remaing gaseous product CO is 2/20 × 28 = 2.8g So, the correct option is (1)

questions.question: Carboxylic acids have higher boiling points than aldehydes, ketones and even alcohols of comparable molecular mass. It is due to their

options.A: More extensive association of carboxylic acid via van der Waals force of attraction

questions.explanation: Sol. Due to formation of intermolecular H- bonding in carboxylic acid, association occurs. Hence boiling point increases and become more than the boiling point of aldehydes, ketones and alcohols of comparable molecular masses.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Which one of the following ions exhibits d- d transition and paramagnetism as well?
questions.explanation: Sol. CrO42- ⇌ Cr6+ = [Ar] Unpaired electron (n) = 0 ; Diamagnetic Cr2O72- ⇌ Cr6+ = [Ar] Unpaired electron (n) = 0 ; Diamagnetic MnO42- = Mn6+ = [Ar]3d¹ Unpaired electron (n) = 1 ; Paramagnetic MnO4- = Mn7+ = [Ar] Unpaired electron (n) = 0 ; Diamagnetic

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Iron carbonyl, Fe(CO)5 is
questions.explanation: Sol. Based on the number of metal atoms present in a complex, they are classified into monuclear, dinuclear, trinuclear and so on. eg: Fe(CO)5 : monuclear Co2(CO)8 : dinuclear Fe3(CO)12 : trinuclear Hence, option (2) should be the right answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The type of isomerism shown by the complex [CoCl2(en)2] is
questions.explanation: Sol. In [CoCl2(en)2], Coordination number of Co is 6 and this compound has octahedral geometry. As per given option, type of isomerism is geometrical isomerism.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The geometry and magnetic behaviour of the complex [Ni(CO)4] are
questions.explanation: Sol. Ni(28):[Ar]3d⁸ 4s² : CO is a strong field ligand Configuration would be: For, four CO' - ligands hybridisation would be sp³ and thus the complex would be diamagnetic and of tetrahedral geometry.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Following solutions were prepared by mixing different volumes of NaOH and HCl of different concentrations: a. 60 mL M/10 HCl + 40 mL M/10 NaOH b. 55 mL M/10 HCl + 45 mL M/10 NaOH c. 75 mL M/5 HCl + 25 mL M/5 NaOH d. 100 mL M/10 HCl + 100 mL M/10 NaOH pH of which one of them will be equal to 1?
questions.explanation: Sol. Meq of HCl = 75 × 1/5 × 1 = 15 Meq of NaOH = 25 × 1/5 × 1 = 5 Meq of HCl in resulting solution = 10 Molarity of [H+] in resulting mixture = 10/100 = 1/10 pH = -log[H+] = -log[1/10] = 1.0

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: On which of the following properties does the coagulating power of an ion depend?

options.A: Both magnitude and sign of the charge on the ion
options.C: The magnitude of the charge on the ion alone
options.D: The sign of charge on the ion alone

questions.explanation: Sol. Coagulation of colloidal solution by using an electrolyte depends on the charge present (positive or negative) on colloidal particles as well as on its size. Coagulating power of an electrolyte depends on the magnitude of charge present on effective ion of electrolyte.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Given van der Waals constant for NH₃, H₂, O₂ and CO₂ are respectively 4.17, 0.244, 1.36 and 3.59, which one of the following gases is most easily liquefied?
questions.explanation: Sol. van der waal constant 'a', signifies intermolecular forces of attraction. Higher is the value of 'a', easier will be the liquefaction of gas.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The solubility of BaSO4 in water is 2.42 × 10⁻3 g L-1 at 298 K. The value of its solubility product (Ksp) will be (Given molar mass of BaSO4 = 233 g mol-1)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: In which case is number of molecules of water maximum?

options.A: 0.00224 L of water vapours at 1 atm and 273 K

questions.explanation: Sol. (1) Moles of water = 0.00224/22.4 = 10-4 Molecules of water = mole x NA = 10-4 NA (2) Molecules of water = mole x NA = 0.18/18 NA = 10-2 NA (3) Mass of water = 18 x 1 = 18 g Molecules of water = mole x NA = 18/18 NA = NA (4) Molecules of water = mole x NA = 10-3 NA

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The correct difference between first and second order reactions is that

options.A: A first-order reaction can catalyzed; a second-order reaction cannot be catalyzed
