options.B: The half-life of a first-order reaction does not depend on [A]o; the half-life of a second-order reaction does depend on [A]o
options.C: The rate of a first-order reaction does not depend on reactant concentrations; the rate of a second-order reaction does depend on reactant concentrations
options.D: The rate of a first-order reaction does depend on reactant concentrations; the rate of a second-order reaction does not depend on reactant concentrations

questions.explanation: Sol. For first order reaction, t1/2 = 0.693/k which is independent of initial concentration of reactant. For second order reaction, t1/2 = 1/k[A]0 which depends on initial concentration of reactant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Among CaH2, BeH2, BaH2 the order of ionic character is
questions.explanation: Sol. For 2nd group hydrides, on moving down the group metallic character of metals increases so ionic character of metal hydride increases. Hence the option (3) should be correct option.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Consider the change in oxidation state of Bromine corresponding to different emf values as shown in the diagram below: BrO4- --1.82V--> BrO3- --1.5V--> HBrO --1.595V--> Br₂ --1.0652V--> Br- Then the species undergoing disproportionation is
questions.explanation: Sol. BrO4- --> Br₂, E0HBrO/Br₂ = 1.595V HBrO --> BrO3-, E0BrO3-/HBrO = 1.5V E0cell for the disproportionation of HBrO E0cell = E0HBrO/Br₂ - E0BrO3-/HBrO = 1.595 - 1.5 = 0.095V = +ve Hence, option (4) is correct answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: For the redox reaction MnO4- + C2O42- + H+ → Mn2+ + CO₂ + H₂O The correct coefficients of the reactants for the balanced equation are MnO4- C2O42- H+
questions.explanation: Sol. n- factor of MnO4- = 5, n- factor of C2O42- = 2 Ratio of n- factors of MnO4- and C2O42- is 5 : 2 So, molar ratio in balanced reaction is 2 : 5 : 16 The balanced equation is 2MnO4- + 5C2O42- + 16H+ → 2Mn2+ + 10CO2 + 8H2O

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Which one of the following conditions will favour maximum formation of the product in the reaction, A2(g) + B2(g) ⇌ X2(g) ΔrH = -X kJ?
questions.explanation: Sol. On increasing pressure equilibrium shifts in a direction where pressure decreases i.e. forward direction. On decreasing temperature, equilibrium shifts in exothermic direction i.e., forward direction. So, high pressure and low temperature favours maximum formation of product.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: When initial concentration of the reactant is doubled, the half-life period of a zero order reaction
questions.explanation: Sol. Half life of zero order t1/2 = [A0]/2K t1/2 will be doubled on doubling the initial concentration.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The bond dissociation energies of X2, Y2 and XY are in the ratio of 1 : 0.5 : 1. ΔH for the formation of XY is -200 kJ mol-1. The bond dissociation energy of X2 will be
questions.explanation: Sol. The reaction for ΔrH°(XY) 1/2 X2(g) + 1/2 Y2(g) → XY(g) Bond energies of X2, Y2 and XY are X, X/2, X respectively ∴ ΔH = (X/2 + X/4) - X = -200 On solving, we get -X/2 + X/4 = -200 ⇒ -X/4 = -200 ⇒ X = 800 kJ/mol

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The correction factor 'a' to the ideal gas equation corresponds to

options.A: Electric field present between the gas molecules
options.D: Forces of attraction between the gas molecules

questions.explanation: Sol. In real gas equation, (P + an2/V2)(V - nb) = nRT van der Waal's constant, 'a' signifies intermolecular forces of attraction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Consider the following species: CN+, CN-, NO and CN. Which one of these will have the highest bond order?
questions.explanation: Sol. NO : BO = (10-5)/2 = 2.5 CN- : BO = (10-4)/2 = 3 CN : BO = (9-4)/2 = 2.5 CN+ : BO = (8-4)/2 = 2 Hence, option(2) should be the right answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Magnesium reacts with an element (X) to form an ionic compound. If the ground state electronic configuration of (X) is 1s² 2s² 2p³, the simplest formula for this compound is
questions.explanation: Sol. Element (X) electronic configuration 1s² 2s² 2p³ So, valency of X will be 3. Valency of Mg is 2. Formula of compound formed by Mg and X will be Mg3X2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Iron exhibits bcc structure at room temperature. Above 900°C, it transforms to fcc structure. The ratio of density of iron at room temperature to that at 900°C (assuming molar mass and atomic radii of iron remains constant with temperature) is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Oxygen is not produced during photosynthesis by
questions.explanation: Sol. Green sulphur bacteria do not use H₂O as source of proton, therefore they do not evolve O₂

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Double fertilization is

options.A: Fusion of two male gametes with one egg
options.B: Fusion of one male gamete with two polar nuclei
options.C: Fusion of two male gametes of a pollen tube with two different eggs

questions.explanation: Sol. Double fertilization is a unique phenomenon that occur in angiosperms only. Syngamy + Triple fusion = Double fertilization

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the reaction H–C≡CH →(1)NaNH2/liq.NH₃(2)CH3CH2Br X →(1)NaNH2/liq.NH₃(2)CH3CH2Br Y X and Y are :
questions.explanation: [Reaction scheme showing: H-C≡CH --(NaNH2, liq. NH₃)--> H-C≡C-Na⁺ --(CH3CH2Br)--> H-C≡C-CH2CH3 (X, 1-Butyne)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The rate of a first-order reaction is 0.04 mol l^{-1}s^{-1} at 10 seconds and 0.03 mol l^{-1}s^{-1} at 20 seconds after initiation of the reaction. The half-life period of the reaction is :

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is correct when SO₂ is passed through acidified K2Cr2O7 solution ?
questions.explanation: K2Cr2O7+SO₂+H₂SO₄ →K2SO4+Cr2(SO₄)3+H₂O green colour

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When copper is heated with conc. HNO₃ it produces
questions.explanation: Cu+4HNO3 (conc.) →Cu(NO₃)2+2NO2+2H2O

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The pressure of H₂ required to make the potential of H₂ electrode zero in pure water at 298 K is :-
questions.explanation: 2H⁺(aq) + 2e⁻ → H₂(g) P_H₂ = 10⁻¹⁴ atm

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The ionic radii of A⁺ and B⁻ ions are 0.98 × 10⁻¹⁰ m and 1.81 × 10⁻¹⁰ m. The coordination number of each ion in AB is :-
questions.explanation: radii ratio = r_s / r_l = (0.98 × 10⁻¹⁰) / (1.81 × 10⁻¹⁰) = 0.54 radii ratio is in between 0.414 to 0.732 so, coordination number is 6

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Which of the following statement about the composition of the vapour over an ideal 1 : 1 molar mixture of benzene and toluene is correct? Assume that the temperature is constant at 25°C. (Given : Vapour Pressure Data at 25°C, benzene = 12.8 kPa, Toluene = 3.85 kPa)

options.A: The vapour will contain a higher percentage of benzene
options.B: The vapour will contain a higher percentage of toluene
options.C: The vapour will contain equal amounts of benzene and toluene
options.D: Not enough information is given to make a predication

questions.explanation: A → benzene, B → toluene 1 : 1 molar mixture of A and B ∴ x_A = 1/2 and x_B = 1/2 P_s = P_A⁰X_A + P_B⁰X_B

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The reaction CH₃-CH₂-Br + AgCN → Product Can be classified as :-
questions.explanation: This is an example of Williamson ether synthesis reaction in which sodium alkoxide reacts with alkyl halide and gives ether.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: For the following reactions :- (a) CH3CH2Br + KOH → CH3CH = CH₂ + KBr + H₂O (b) CH3CH2Br + KOH → CH3CH2OH + KBr (c) CH₂=CH-CH₃ + HBr → CH₃-CHBr-CH₃ Which of the following statements is correct ?

options.A: (a) and (b) are elimination reaction and (c) is addition reaction
options.B: (a) is elimination, (b) is substitution and (c) is addition reaction
options.C: (a) is elimination, (b) and (c) are substitution reactions
options.D: (a) is substitution, (b) and (c) are addition reaction

questions.explanation: (a) CH3CH2Br + KOH → CH3CH = CH₂ + KBr + H₂O breaking of 2σ bonds and formation of 1π bond so it is an example of elimination reaction. (b) CH3CH2Br + KOH → CH3CH2OH + KBr replacement of Br⁻ by OH⁻ is substitution reaction (c) CH₂=CH-CH₃ + HBr → CH₃-CHBr-CH₃ breaking of 1π bond and formation of 2σ bonds is addition reaction

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The pair of electron in the given carbanion, CH₃C≡C⁻, is present in which of the following orbitals ?
questions.explanation: CH₃- C ≡ C⁻ No. of σ bp - 1 & hybridisation is sp, lp - 1

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A sample of ammonium phosphate (NH₄)3PO4 contains 3.18 moles of hydrogen atoms. The number of moles of oxygen atoms in the sample is
questions.explanation: In one mole of (NH₄)3PO4, there are 12 moles of H atoms and 4 moles of O atoms. Therefore, for 3.18 moles of H, the moles of O are 412×3.18=1.06124×3.18=1.06.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The total number of electrons in 1.6g of CH4 to that in 1.8g of H2O
questions.explanation: 1.6g of CH₄ is 0.1 mol, with 10 electrons per molecule, totaling 1 mole of electrons. 1.8g of H₂O is 0.1 mol, with 10 electrons per molecule, also totaling 1 mole of electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When xx molecules are removed from 200mg of N2O 2.89×10−3 moles of N2O are left. xx will be
questions.explanation: The initial number of molecules in 200 mg of N2O is approximately 2.7×1021. The remaing 2.89×10−3 moles correspond to 1.7×1021 molecules. Therefore, x=(2.7×1021)−(1.7×1021)=1021 molecules.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: An isotone of 76 32Ge is
questions.explanation: Isotones are atoms with the same number of neutrons. The number of neutrons in 76 32Ge is 76 - 32 = 44. 77 33As also has 44 neutrons (77 - 33 = 44).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The ratio of specific charge of an electron to that of a proton is
questions.explanation: Specific charge is the charge-to-mass ratio (e/m). An electron and a proton have the same magnitude of charge, but the mass of a proton is approximately 1837 times that of an electron. Therefore, the specific charge of an electron is 1837 times that of a proton.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Atomic number and mass number of an element M are 25 and 52 respectively. The number of electrons, protons and neutrons in M2+ ion are respectively
questions.explanation: Atomic number (protons) = 25, mass number = 52, so neutrons = 52-25 = 27. For M2+, two electrons are removed, so electrons = 25-2 = 23. Protons remain 25. The answer is 23, 25, 27.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The frequency of a wave is 6 × 1015 s–1. Its wave number would be

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of photons of light of wavelength 7000 Å equivalent to 1 J are
questions.explanation: Energy of one photon, E=hcλE=λhc. The number of photons, n=1 JE=λhcn=E1 J=hcλ. Substituting the values, n=7000×10−106.626×10−34×3×108≈3.52×1018n=6.626×10−34×3×1087000×10−10≈3.52×1018.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.topic: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: The valence shell electronic configuration of transition elements is
questions.explanation: Transition elements are d-block elements, characterized by the filling of d orbitals. Their general electronic configuration is (n−1)d¹−10ns⁰−2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.topic: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Eka-aluminium and Eka-silicon are known as
questions.explanation: Mendeleev used the prefix "eka-" for elements predicted by him but not yet discovered. Eka-aluminium was later named gallium, and eka-silicon was named germanium.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.topic: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Which is mismatched regarding the position of the element as given below?
questions.explanation: Element with Z=89 (Actinium, Ac) is a d-block element, not f-block. Element with Z=115 (Moscovium, Mc) is a p-block element, not d-block. Therefore, both options (1) and (3) are mismatched.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.topic: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Modern periodic law is

options.A: The physical and chemical properties of the elements are periodic function of their atomic numbers
options.B: The physical and chemical properties of the elements depend upon the energy of the electrons
options.C: The physical and chemical properties of the elements depend upon atomic wt.

questions.explanation: The modern periodic law, as stated by Henry Moseley, establishes that the properties of elements are a periodic function of their atomic numbers, not their atomic weights.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.topic: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.question: Which of the following statements about modern periodic table is incorrect?

options.A: The p-block has 6 column because a maximum of 6 electrons can occupy all the orbitals in the p-subshell
options.B: The d-block has 8 columns because a maximum of 8 electrons can occupy all the orbitals in the d-subshell
options.C: Each block contains a number of columns equal to number of electrons that can occupy the subshell
options.D: The block indicates the value of azimuthal quantum number for the last subshell which received electrons

questions.explanation: The d-block has 10 columns because a d-subshell has 5 orbitals, each holding a maximum of 2 electrons, for a total of 10 electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct sequence of increasing covalent character is represented by
questions.explanation: According to Fajan's rules, covalent character increases as the size of the cation decreases and the size of the anion increases. The cation size order is Na+ > Li+ > Be2+, so the covalent character order is NaCl < LiCl < BeCl2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The formal charges of oxygen labelled 3,2,1 in ozone respectively are O O 1 2 3
questions.explanation: Using the formula, Formal Charge = Valence electrons - Lone pairs - 1/2(Bonding electrons). For O1 (central), it is 6 - 2 - 3 = +1. For O₂ (double-bonded terminal), it is 6 - 4 - 2 = 0. For O₃ (single-bonded terminal), it is 6 - 6 - 1 = -1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following bonds has the highest bond energy?
questions.explanation: While bond energy generally decreases down a group due to larger atomic size, O-O has a lower bond energy than S-S due to lone pair-lone pair repulsion between the small oxygen atoms. This makes the S-S bond stronger.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: dπ – pπ bond present in
questions.explanation: dπ-pπ bonding occurs when a p-orbital of one atom overlaps with a d-orbital of another atom. Phosphorus has vacant 3d orbitals, allowing for this type of bonding in phosphate ion (PO4^3-), whereas the other ions (CO₃^2-, NO₃-, NO₂-) involve only p-orbitals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A sample of gas occupies 10 L under a pressure of 1 atm. What will be its volume if the pressure is increased to 2 atm? Assuming that temperature of the gas sample does not change
questions.explanation: According to Boyle's Law (P₁V₁ = P₂V₂), at constant temperature, the volume is inversely proportional to pressure. V₂ = (P₁V₁)/P₂ = (1 atm * 10 L) / 2 atm = 5 L.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A gas at a pressure of 5 atm is heated from 0° to 546°C and simultaneously compressed to 1/3 of its original volume. Hence final pressure is
questions.explanation: Using the combined gas law, P₁V₁/T₁ = P₂V₂/T₂. T₁ = 273 K, T₂ = 819 K, V₂ = V₁/3. P₂ = P₁V₁T₂ / (T₁V₂) = (5 * V₁ * 819) / (273 * V₁/3) = 5 * 819 / (273/3) = 5 * 819 * 3 / 273 = 45 atm.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: How much should the pressure be increased in order to decrease the volume of a gas by 5% at a constant temperature?
questions.explanation: Applying Boyle's Law, P₁V₁ = P₂V₂. If V₂ = 0.95 V₁, then P₂ = P₁V₁ / (0.95V₁) = P₁/0.95 ≈ 1.0526 P₁. The percentage increase in pressure is (1.0526 - 1) * 100% = 5.26%.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When the temperature is raised through 1°C the volume is increased by 1/273 times of the original volume. This is
questions.explanation: Charles' Law states that at constant pressure, the volume of a fixed mass of a gas is directly proportional to its absolute temperature. The given description is the original statement of Charles' Law for a 1°C change at 0°C.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which curve shows Charles’ law?
questions.explanation: Charles' Law states V ∝ T, so V = kT. Rearranging, V * (1/T) = k. A plot of V versus 1/T will give a straight line with a negative slope? Actually, a plot of V vs T is a straight line with positive slope. The graph of V vs 1/T is a hyperbola.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Tea placed in thermos flask is an example of
questions.explanation: A thermos flask is designed to prevent the exchange of both matter and energy with the surroundings, making the tea inside an isolated system.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Gaseous system is placed with pressure P1, volume V1 and temperature T1, it has undergone thermodynamic changes where temperature is remaing constant, it is
questions.explanation: An isothermal process is defined as a thermodynamic process in which the temperature of the system remains constant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A thermally isolated, gaseous system can exchange energy with the surroundings. The mode of energy may be
questions.explanation: A thermally isolated system is one that does not exchange heat with its surroundings (q = 0). However, it can still exchange energy in the form of work (w).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a state function?
questions.explanation: Heat (q) and work (w) are path functions, meaning their values depend on how a process is carried out. Their sum (q + w) equals the change in internal energy (ΔU), which is a state function.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The Kc for given reaction will be 2 A (g) 2B(g) C(g) 2D(s) + ���⇀ ↽���
questions.explanation: The equilibrium constant Kc is the ratio of product concentrations to reactant concentrations, each raised to their stoichiometric coefficients. Solids are not included in the expression. Thus, Kc = [C][A2][B]2[C].

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For which of the following reaction, the degree of dissociation (α) and equilibrium constant (Kp) are related as Kp=4α2P1−α2Kp=1−α24α2P ?
questions.explanation: For the dissociation reaction N2O4(g) ⇌ 2NO2(g), if α is the degree of dissociation, the total moles at equilibrium is 1+α. The partial pressures lead to the given Kp expression. This relation is specific to reactions with a change in the number of moles.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following does the reaction go almost to completion?
questions.explanation: A very large equilibrium constant (Kc >> 1) indicates that the reaction strongly favors the products, meaning it proceeds almost to completion. 10^3 is the largest value among the options.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In a chemical equilibrium the rate constant of the backward reaction is 7.5 × 10–4 and the equilibrium constant is 1.5. So the rate constant of the forward reaction is
questions.explanation: For an elementary reaction, the equilibrium constant K is the ratio of the rate constant of the forward reaction (Kf) to the rate constant of the backward reaction (Kb). Therefore, Kf = K * Kb = 1.5 × 7.5 × 10–4 = 1.125 × 10–3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The process in which oxidation number increases is
questions.explanation: Oxidation is defined as an increase in oxidation number (loss of electrons).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The oxidation number of phosphorus vary from
questions.explanation: Phosphorus has 5 valence electrons. Its common oxidation states range from -3 (e.g., in phosphine, PH3) to +5 (e.g., in phosphoric acid, H3PO4).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Oxidation number of iodine varies from
questions.explanation: Iodine, being a halogen, has 7 valence electrons. Its oxidation states can range from -1 (e.g., I⁻) to +7 (e.g., in periodate, IO4⁻).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Oxidation number of oxygen atom in O₃ molecule is
questions.explanation: In an elemental form like ozone (O₃), which consists of only one type of atom, the oxidation number of each atom is zero.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The oxidation number of C in CH₄, CH3Cl, CH2Cl2, CHCl3 and CCl4 is respectively
questions.explanation: Assigning oxidation numbers (x) using standard rules (H = +1, Cl = -1). For CH₄: x + 4(+1) = 0, x = -4. For CH3Cl: x + 3(+1) + (-1) = 0, x = -2. This pattern leads to the sequence -4, -2, 0, +2, +4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is not a method of preparation of dihydrogen?

options.A: Reaction of granulated zinc with dil HCl
options.B: Electrolysis of acidified water using platinum electrodes
options.C: Reaction of zinc with conc. HNO₃
options.D: Reaction of aluminum with aqueous NaOH

questions.explanation: Concentrated nitric acid is a strong oxidizing agent. It does not liberate hydrogen gas with metals; instead, it oxidizes the hydrogen to water and itself gets reduced to nitrogen oxides.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Heavy water is used as a
questions.explanation: Heavy water (D2O) is an excellent moderator in nuclear reactors because it can slow down fast neutrons to thermal energies without absorbing them significantly.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect for hydrogen?
questions.explanation: Of the three isotopes of hydrogen (protium, deuterium, and tritium), only tritium is radioactive. The statement "its one isotope is radioactive" is correct. Therefore, the option claiming this is incorrect is false.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Nascent hydrogen consists of
questions.explanation: Nascent hydrogen refers to hydrogen gas at the moment of its generation, which is believed to be in the atomic state (H) and therefore more reactive than molecular hydrogen (H₂).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The alkali metals, which element has the highest ionization enthalpy?
questions.explanation: Ionization enthalpy decreases down a group in the periodic table. Lithium is at the top of Group 1, so it has the highest ionization enthalpy among the alkali metals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Sodium peroxide is used in the purification of air in submarines because it
questions.explanation: Sodium peroxide (Na2O2) reacts with carbon dioxide (CO₂) exhaled by humans to produce oxygen (O₂) and sodium carbonate, thus helping to regenerate breathable air. The reaction is 2Na2O2 + 2CO2 → 2Na2CO3 + O₂.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Plaster of Paris is obtained by
questions.explanation: Plaster of Paris (CaSO4·1/2H2O) is produced by heating gypsum (CaSO4·2H2O) to a temperature of about 120°C - 130°C. This process drives off three-quarters of the water of crystallization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is used as a drying agent?
questions.explanation: Calcium oxide (CaO), also known as quicklime, is a basic anhydride. It reacts readily with water to form calcium hydroxide, making it an effective drying agent for solvents.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect for diborane?

options.C: Each boron atom is sp³ hybridized

questions.explanation: In diborane (B2H6), each boron atom forms four bonds but is not sp³ hybridized. It uses sp³ hybrid orbitals for bonding, but the structure involves two 3-center-2-electron (bana) bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following oxides of nitrogen is called laughing gas?
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide (N2O) is commonly known as laughing gas due to its euphoric effects when inhaled. It is used as an anesthetic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Phosphorus exists in which of the following allotropic forms?
questions.explanation: Phosphorus has several allotropes, the most common being white phosphorus, red phosphorus, and black phosphorus. White phosphorus is highly reactive, while black phosphorus is the most stable.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is used in the manufacture of safety matches?
questions.explanation: Red phosphorus is used in the striking surface of safety matchboxes due to its low reactivity and stability. White phosphorus is too reactive and dangerous for such use.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Which of the following is heterocyclic compound?
questions.explanation: Heterocyclic compounds are cyclic compounds that contain atoms of at least two different elements as members of its ring(s). Pyrrole (with N), Furan (with O), and Thiophene (with S) are all classic examples of heterocyclic compounds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: Which of the following shows structure of neohexane?
questions.explanation: Neohexane is a common name for 2,2-dimethylbutane. Its structure has a central carbon (C2) bonded to two methyl groups, an ethyl group, and a hydrogen atom. Option C represents this structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The incorrect match is
questions.explanation: The general formula for acyclic saturated hydrocarbons (alkanes) is CnH2n+2. Bicycloalkanes, which are saturated and contain rings, have the general formula CnH2n-2, not CnH2n+2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: The number of C atoms present in allyl vinyl acetylene is ___ and it has ___ degree of unsaturation
questions.explanation: Allyl vinyl acetylene is CH₂=CH-CH₂-C≡C-CH=CH₂. It has 7 carbon atoms. Degree of unsaturation can be calculated from the formula: for C7H8, DU = (2*7 + 2 - 8)/2 = 4, which corresponds to the three double bonds and one triple bond present.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The difference in potential energy between eclipsed and staggered form of ethane is
questions.explanation: The staggered conformation of ethane is more stable than the eclipsed conformation. The energy difference between these two conformations, known as torsional strain, is approximately 12.55 kJ/mol (or 3.0 kcal/mol).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Eclipsed form of ethane has higher energy due to
questions.explanation: The higher energy of the eclipsed form of ethane is primarily due to torsional strain, which is the repulsion between the electron clouds of the C-H bonds as they pass by each other in the eclipsed conformation. Steric strain is more significant for larger groups.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The angle strain in cyclopentane is
questions.explanation: Angle strain is the deviation from the ideal tetrahedral bond angle of 109.5°. For a planar cyclopentane, the internal angle would be 108°. The deviation, and thus the angle strain, is (109.5° - 108°) / 2 = 0.75° = 45'. The provided answer is 44'.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one is most stable?
questions.explanation: Cyclohexane is the most stable of these cycloalkanes. It adopts a non-planar chair conformation that is virtually free of angle strain, unlike the smaller rings which have significant angle strain.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The chair form is __ stable than boat form by potential energy __ kJ/mol.
questions.explanation: The chair conformation of cyclohexane is more stable than the boat conformation by about 44 kJ/mol due to the absence of torsional and steric strain present in the boat form (like flagpole interactions).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a secondary alkyl halide?
questions.explanation: In isopropyl chloride (CH₃-CHCl-CH₃), the carbon atom bonded to the chlorine atom is also bonded to two other carbon atoms and one hydrogen atom, making it a secondary (2°) carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of the compound CH Br 3 2 is
questions.explanation: The longest carbon chain includes the double bond, giving a four-carbon chain (but). The double bond gets priority in numbering, so it starts at C1. The bromine is on C1, and the double bond is between C2 and C3, making the name 1-bromobut-2-ene.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: IUPAC name of HOCH2 OH is:
questions.explanation: The IUPAC name for HOCH2OH is ethane-1,2-diol. The parent chain is ethane, and the two hydroxyl groups are on carbons 1 and 2, hence the suffix "-diol" with locants.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The general formula of both aldehyde and ketone is
questions.explanation: Both aldehydes and ketones contain one carbonyl group (C=O). For a compound with one carbonyl and no other unsaturation, the general formula is CnH2nO. Formaldehyde (CH2O) and acetaldehyde (C2H4O) are examples.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of following structure is 3 2 CH | CH — CH — C— CH — CH OH || O
questions.explanation: The correct IUPAC name is 1-hydroxy-4-methylpentan-3-one. The longest chain includes both the alcohol and ketone functional groups, with the alcohol (-OH) given higher priority for the suffix. The ketone is then treated as a substituent (oxo) or with the suffix -one.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: IUPAC name of CCl3CHO is
questions.explanation: The IUPAC name for CCl3CHO is 2,2-trichloroethanal. The parent chain is ethanal (2 carbons including the carbonyl carbon). The three chlorine atoms are all on the second carbon (C2), hence the name.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is mixed ketone?
questions.explanation: A mixed or unsymmetrical ketone is one where the two alkyl/aryl groups attached to the carbonyl carbon are different. Acetophenone (C6H5-CO-CH₃) is a mixed ketone, with a phenyl and a methyl group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the following reaction product P is 2 4 H Pd BaSO O || R — C— Cl P － ——
questions.explanation: This is the Rosenmund reduction. Hydrogen gas in the presence of a poisoned palladium catalyst (Pd/BaSO4) selectively reduces an acyl chloride (R-CO-Cl) to an aldehyde (R-CHO) without further reducing it to a primary alcohol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: CH C N 3 H CH₃ is a
questions.explanation: The given compound has the structure CH₃-CH(NH2)-CH₂-CH₃. The nitrogen is bonded to one carbon atom (from the ethyl chain) and two hydrogens, making it a primary amine.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct IUPAC name for CH NH CH 2 3 is
questions.explanation: The compound CH₂=CH-CH₂-NH-CH₃ has a propene chain with the amine group on C1. The amine is secondary (N-methyl), so the IUPAC name is N-methylprop-2-en-1-amine.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The compound having the molecular formula C3H9N can represent
questions.explanation: C3H9N can represent several isomeric amines: propylamine (n-propylamine), isopropylamine, and trimethylamine. All are structural isomers with this molecular formula.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A secondary amine is

options.B: A compound with two carbon atoms and a –NH2 group
options.C: A compound with a –NH2 group on the carbon atom in number two position
options.D: A compound in which two of the hydrogens of NH₃ have been replaced by alkyl or aryl groups

questions.explanation: A secondary amine is derived from ammonia (NH₃) by replacing two of its hydrogen atoms with alkyl or aryl groups. Its general formula is R2NH, where R can be alkyl or aryl.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a synthetic polymer?
questions.explanation: Synthetic polymers are man-made. Polyethylene is a common synthetic polymer produced from the polymerization of ethylene. Starch and natural rubber are natural, and cellulose acetate is semi-synthetic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a linear polymer?
questions.explanation: High-density polythene (HDPE) consists of linear polymer chains with little to no branching. This linear structure allows for tight packing and high density. Low-density polythene is branched.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a cross linked polymer?
questions.explanation: Cross-linked polymers have strong covalent bonds between different polymer chains, forming a three-dimensional network. Bakelite is a classic example of a cross-linked polymer, which makes it hard and infusible (thermosetting).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Chemotherapy is

options.A: Use of heat for therapeutic effect
options.B: Use of chemical for therapeutic effect
options.C: Use of natural reagent for therapeutic effect

questions.explanation: Chemotherapy is the treatment of disease by the use of chemical substances, especially in the context of cancer treatment where chemicals are used to kill or inhibit the growth of cancer cells.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: How do enzyme catalyse the reaction?

options.A: By holding the substrate molecule for chemical reaction
options.B: By providing functional groups which will attack the substrate to carry out chemical reaction

questions.explanation: Enzymes catalyze reactions by providing a specific active site that holds the substrate(s) in the correct orientation. They also contain functional groups (e.g., from amino acid side chains) that can participate in the chemical reaction, stabilizing transition states.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Drugs which compete with natural substrate for their attachment on the active sites of enzymes are called
questions.explanation: Competive inhibitors are drugs that structurally resemble the natural substrate and compete with it for binding to the active site of an enzyme, thereby reducing the enzyme's activity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Drugs which interfere with natural action of histamine by competing with histamine are called
questions.explanation: Antihistamines are drugs that block the action of histamine, a compound released during allergic reactions, by competing with it for binding to histamine receptors.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When x molecules are removed from 200 mg of N O, 2.89 × 10–3 moles of N O are left. x will be 2 2
questions.explanation: From Equation – = (200 mg of NO) – x molecules = 2.89 × 10–3 moles of NO 2 2 200 ×10−3×6.022×1023 200 mg of N O have molecule = 2 44 ≈ 2.7 × 1021 molecule ∴ 2.89 × 10-3 moles of N O have molecule = 2.89 × 10-3 × 6.022 × 1023 2 = 1.7 × 1021 molecules ∴ 200 mg of N O – x molecule = 2.89 × 10–3 moles of N O 2 2 [2.7 × 1021 – x = 1.7 × 1021] molecule [x = (2.7 - 1.7) × 1021] molecule = 1021 molecule

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A mixture of gases contains H and O gases in the ratio of 1 : 4 (w/w). What is the molar ratio of the two 2 2 gases in the mixture?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When 22.4 litres of H (g) is mixed with 11.2 litres of Cl (g), each at STP, the moles of HCl(g) formed is equal to 2 2
questions.explanation: H +Cl ⎯⎯→2HCl 2 2 n n 11.2 n Cl₂ = HCl = HCl 1 2 22.4 2 n =1 HCl

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: How many grams of CH OH should be added to water to prepare 150 ml solution of 2 M CH OH? 3 3
questions.explanation: M×VmL 2×150 = Moles of CH OH = = 0.3 mole 3 1000 1000 ∴ weight of CH OH = moles × mol. mass 3 = 0.3×32=9.6g

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In Haber process 30 litres of dihydrogen and 30 litres of dinitrogen were taken for reaction which yielded only 50% of the expected product. What will be the composition of gaseous mixture under the aforesaid condition in the end?

options.A: 20 litres ammonia, 20 litres nitrogen, 20 litres hydrogen
options.B: 10 litres ammonia, 25 litres nitrogen, 15 litres hydrogen
options.C: 20 litres ammonia, 10 litres nitrogen, 30 litres hydrogen
options.D: 20 litres ammonia, 25 litres nitrogen, 15 litres hydrogen

questions.explanation: H₂ is LR. 50% yield = 10L NH₃. N₂ left = 30-5 = 25L. H₂ left = 30-15 = 15L.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The total number of electrons in 2.0 g of D2O to that in 1.8 g of H₂O
questions.explanation: Both have 0.1 mol, and both D2O and H₂O have 10 electrons per molecule. So equal number of electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: From 200 mg of CO₂ when x molecules are removed, 2.89 × 10-3 moles of CO₂ are left. x will be

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the weight of metal oxide is x g containg y g of oxygen, the equivalent weight of metal will be
questions.explanation: Metal weight = (x-y)g. Eq. wt = (x-y)/y × 8 = 8(x-y)/y

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The frequency of a wave is 6 × 1015 s⁻¹. Its wave number would be

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The threshold energy is given as E₀ and radiation of energy E falls on metal, then K.E. is given as
questions.explanation: Einstein's photoelectric equation: K.E. = E - E₀

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to Bohr's theory angular momentum of an electron in 6th orbit is
questions.explanation: Angular momentum = nh/2π = 6h/2π = 3h/π

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If r1 is the radius of the first orbit of hydrogen atom, then the radii of second, third and fourth orbits in terms of r1 are

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Electronic energy is negative because

options.B: Energy is zero near the nucleus and decreases as the distance from nucleus increases
options.C: Energy is zero at infinite distance from the nucleus and decreases as the electron comes towards nucleus

questions.explanation: At infinity, PE=0. As electron approaches nucleus, energy decreases (becomes more negative).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: An electron jumps from lower orbit to higher orbit, when
questions.explanation: Energy must be absorbed for an electron to jump to a higher energy level.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the energy difference between the ground state and excited state of an atom is 4.4 × 10-19 J. The wavelength of photon required to produce this transition is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The ionization energy of the electron in the lowest orbit of hydrogen atom is 13.6 eV. The energies required in eV to remove electron from three lowest orbits of hydrogen atom are

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which transition of Li2+ is associated with same energy change as n = 6 to n = 4 transition in He+?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Zeeman effect refers to the

options.A: Splitting of the spectral lines in a magnetic field
options.B: Splitting up of the spectral lines in an electrostatic field
options.C: Emission of electrons from metals when light falls on it
options.D: Random scattering of α-particles by gold foil

questions.explanation: Zeeman effect is the splitting of spectral lines in a magnetic field.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assuming the velocity to be same, the wavelength of the waves associated with which of the following particles would be maximum?
questions.explanation: λ = h/mv. For same v, λ ∝ 1/m. Electron has least mass, so maximum λ.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the uncertainty in the position of electron is zero, the uncertainty in its momentum would be
questions.explanation: Heisenberg uncertainty: Δx·Δp ≥ h/4π. If Δx=0, Δp = ∞.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If kinetic energy of a proton is increased nine times, the wavelength of the de-Broglie wave associated with it would become

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The momentum of a particle which has a de-Broglie wavelength of 0.1 nm is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The uncertainty in velocity of an electron present in the nucleus of diameter 10-15 m hypothetically should be approximately

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The set of quantum numbers not applicable to an electron
questions.explanation: l cannot equal n. n=1, l=1 is not allowed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The principal and azimuthal quantum number of electrons in 4f orbitals are

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of increasing energy of atomic orbital is
questions.explanation: By (n+l): 5p=6, 6s=6, 4f=7, 5d=7. Same n+l: lower n wins. 5p<6s and 4f<5d. Order: 5p<6s<4f<5d

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which shell would be the first to have 'g' sub-shell?
questions.explanation: g: l=4. Minimum n = l+1 = 5. n=5 is O shell (K=1, L=2, M=3, N=4, O=5).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For which one of the following set of quantum numbers an electron will have the highest energy?
questions.explanation: n+l: (1)=5, (2)=6 (max), (3)=5, (4)=5. Option 2 has highest n+l = 6.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The energies of orbitals of H-atom are in the order
questions.explanation: H atom has 1 electron. All subshells of same n are degenerate. 3s=3p=3d < 4s=4p.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following set of quantum number is possible?
questions.explanation: Option 1: s=-2 invalid. Option 2: l=n=4 invalid. Option 4: s=0 invalid. Option 3: all valid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the presence of magnetic field, the possible number of orientations for an orbital of azimuthal quantum number 3, is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a 'p' electron, the orbital angular momentum is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following electronic level would allow the hydrogen to absorb a photon but not emit a photon?
questions.explanation: 1s is ground state. Can absorb (go up) but cannot emit (already lowest energy level).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following transition will emit maximum energy in hydrogen atom?
questions.explanation: Energy depends only on principal quantum numbers n=4→n=2 in all cases. Same ΔE for all.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): 1 a.m.u. = 1.66 × 10-24 gram.
Reason (R): Actual mass of one atom of C-12 is equal to 1.99 × 10-23 g.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Unit of specific gravity is gram-cc-1.
Reason (R): Specific gravity is same as density of a liquid in normal conditions.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Both A and R are incorrect. Specific gravity is dimensionless.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Number of atoms in 2 mole of NH₃ is equal to number of atoms in 4 mole of CH₄.
Reason (R): Both are chemically similar species.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: NH₃: 8N0 atoms. CH₄: 20N0 atoms. Both A and R are incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): In the reaction 2NaOH + H₃PO₄ → Na2HPO4 + 2H2O, equivalent weight of H₃PO₄ is M/2, where M is its molecular weight.
Reason (R): Equivalent weight = Molecular weight / n-factor.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: n-factor = 2. Equivalent mass = M/2. Both A and R are correct, R explains A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Mass of 1 gram molecule of H₂SO₄ is 98 gram.
Reason (R): One gram atom contains NA atoms.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Both are correct but R is not the explanation of A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): One mole of sucrose reacts completely with oxygen produces 268.8 litre of carbon dioxide at STP.
Reason (R): Amount of oxygen required for reaction is 268.8 litre.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: C12H22O11 + 12O2 → 12CO2 + 11H2O. Both CO₂ and O₂ = 268.8 L. Both correct but R doesn't explain A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): In the reaction 2NaOH + H₂SO₄ → Na₂SO₄ + 2H2O, equivalents of NaOH, Na₂SO₄ and H₂SO₄ are equal.
Reason (R): Number of equivalents = number of moles × n-factor.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Both A and R are correct and R explains A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): When 4 moles of H₂ reacts with 2 moles of O₂, then 4 moles of water is formed.
Reason (R): O₂ will act as limiting reagent.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: 4 mol H₂O formed (A correct). H₂ is the limiting reagent not O₂ (R wrong).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): 50 ml decinormal HCl when mixed with 50 ml decinormal H₂SO₄, then normality of H+ ion in resultant solution is 0.1 N.
Reason (R): Here, MV = M1V1 + M2V2.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Normality = 0.1 N (A correct). Formula should be N1V1+N2V2=N3(V1+V2) (R wrong).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): 50 ml decimolar H₂SO₄ when mixed with 50 ml decimolar NaOH, then normality of resultant solution is 0.05 N.
Reason (R): Here, NV = |N1V1 - N2V2|.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Nsolution = (10-5)/100 = 0.05 N. Both A and R correct, R explains A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Ratio of empirical formula mass and molecular formula mass may be a whole number.
Reason (R): Molecular formula mass = n × empirical formula mass, where n is the simplest whole number.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): For a given solution (density = 1 gm/ml), molality is greater than molarity.
Reason (R): Molarity involves volume of solution while molality involves mass of solvent.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Both A and R are correct and R explains A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): 1 gram of salt in 1 m₃ of solution has concentration of 1 ppm.
Reason (R): ppm is defined as number of parts by mass of solute per million parts of solution.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: ppm = 1 ppm. Both A and R correct, R explains A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Total charge on NA ions of CO₃(2-) is 1.93 × 105 coulomb.
Reason (R): Charge on one electron is 96500 coulomb.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: Charge = 2 × 96500 = 1.93 × 10^5 C (A true). 96500 C is per mole not per electron (R false).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Number of ions in 9 gram of NH₄+ is equal to Avogadro's number (NA).
Reason (R): Number of ions is equal to number of atoms.

options.A: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is correct but R is wrong
options.D: Both A and R are incorrect

questions.explanation: 9g/18 = 0.5 mol = 0.5 NA, not NA. Both A and R incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the ratio of specific heats of a gas is γ, the change in internal energy of a mass of gas, when the volume changes from V to 2V at constant pressure P, is
questions.explanation: ΔU = nCvΔT = n(R/γ-1)ΔT ΔT = T2 - T1 = P(2V)/nR - PV/nR = PV/nR ΔU = n * (R/γ-1) * (PV/nR) = PV/(γ-1)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If for a gas R/Cv = 0.67, which type of gas is this?
questions.explanation: R/Cv = γ - 1 γ - 1 = 0.67 => γ = 1.67. This is the value for a monatomic gas.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A gas is compressed isothermally. The rms velocity of its molecules
questions.explanation: vrms = √(3RT/M). Temperature is constant in an isothermal process, so vrms remains same.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A gas expands such that its pressure and volume obey the law PVn = constant. For the gas to have the largest cooling effect, the value of n should be
questions.explanation: For an adiabatic expansion (n = γ), the cooling is maximum as no heat is absorbed from the surroundings.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is not a state function?
questions.explanation: Work is a path function, not a state function.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The efficiency of a Carnot engine working between 27°C and 127°C is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the door of a working refrigerator is kept open in a closed room, the temperature of the room will
questions.explanation: The refrigerator extracts heat from inside its cabinet and rejects more heat to the room. The net effect is to warm the room.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In a cyclic process, the work done by the system is

options.C: equal to change in internal energy
options.D: equal to the area enclosed by the cycle on P-V diagram

questions.explanation: In a cyclic process, ΔU = 0, so Q = W. The work done is the area enclosed on the P-V diagram.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is correct for an isothermal process?
questions.explanation: For an isothermal process, temperature is constant, so internal energy change (ΔU) is zero for an ideal gas.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In an adiabatic process, the quantity which remains constant is
questions.explanation: In an adiabatic process, Q = 0, so total heat content remains constant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a free expansion of an ideal gas in an insulated container, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: Free expansion is adiabatic (Q=0) and no work is done (W=0). For an ideal gas, internal energy depends only on temperature, so ΔU=0 and temperature remains constant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Joule-Thomson coefficient is zero for
questions.explanation: Joule-Thomson coefficient is zero for an ideal or perfect gas.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The work done in an adiabatic compression of 2 moles of a diatomic gas from 300 K to 400 K is (R = 8.31 J/mol-K)
questions.explanation: W = -ΔU = -nCvΔT = -2 * (5/2)R * 100 = -5 * 8.31 * 100 = -4155 J. Work done on the gas is +4155 J.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A Carnot engine takes 3000 J of heat from a reservoir at 600 K and does some work. The heat rejected to the sink at 300 K is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molar heat capacity of a diatomic gas at constant volume is (in terms of R)
questions.explanation: For a diatomic gas at ordinary temperatures, Cv = (5/2)R.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The internal energy of one mole of a monatomic gas at temperature T is
questions.explanation: For a monatomic gas, U = (3/2)RT.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The ratio Cp/Cv for a monatomic gas is
questions.explanation: For monatomic gas, Cp/Cv = (5/2)R / (3/2)R = 5/3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The first law of thermodynamics is a special case of
questions.explanation: First law of thermodynamics is based on the conservation of energy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In an isothermal expansion of an ideal gas, the amount of heat absorbed is equal to
questions.explanation: ΔU = 0, so Q = W.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: An ideal gas heat engine operates between 127°C and 27°C and absorbs 6 × 104 J of heat. The work done is
questions.explanation: W = Q1 (1 - T2/T1) = 6×104 (1 - 300/400) = 6×104 × 1/4 = 1.5×104 J. Their answer is 1.2×104. Possibly a calculation error.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A refrigerator with coefficient of performance 5 extracts heat from the cooling compartment at the rate of 250 J per cycle. The work done per cycle is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is not a thermodynamic function?
questions.explanation: Work is not a state function, it is a path function.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The entropy change of the universe is
questions.explanation: For a reversible process, the total entropy change of the universe is zero.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Gibbs free energy G is defined as
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction to be spontaneous at all temperatures, which of the following must be true?
questions.explanation: ΔG = ΔH - TΔS. For spontaneity at all temperatures, ΔH must be negative and ΔS positive.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The enthalpy of formation of a compound is defined as

options.A: the heat absorbed when one mole of the compound is formed from its elements in their standard states
options.B: the heat released when one mole of the compound is formed from its elements in their standard states
options.C: the heat absorbed when one mole of the compound is decomposed into its elements
options.D: the heat released when one mole of the compound is burned in oxygen

questions.explanation: Enthalpy of formation is the heat released (or absorbed) when one mole of a compound is formed from its elements in their standard states.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Hess's law is based on the principle that

options.C: Gibbs free energy is a state function
options.D: internal energy is a state function

questions.explanation: Hess's law states that the enthalpy change for a reaction is independent of the path, which is true because enthalpy is a state function.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If C(s) + O2(g) → CO2(g); ΔH = -x kJ CO(g) + 1/2 O2(g) → CO2(g); ΔH = -y kJ Then the heat of formation of CO is
questions.explanation: Target: C(s) + 1/2 O2(g) → CO(g) ; ΔHf = ? Eqn (1) - Eqn (2) gives: C(s) + 1/2 O2(g) → CO(g) with ΔH = -x - (-y) = y - x. Their answer is x - y. Sign confusion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction, ΔH = +20 kJ and ΔS = +40 J/K. The temperature at which the reaction will be spontaneous is
questions.explanation: ΔG = ΔH - TΔS. For spontaneity, ΔG < 0 => 200 - T(40) < 0 => T > 500 K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is true for an exothermic reaction?

options.D: ΔH can be positive or negative

questions.explanation: For an exothermic reaction, heat is released, so ΔH is negative.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The bond dissociation energy of H2 is 435 kJ/mol. The enthalpy of atomization of H2 is
questions.explanation: Enthalpy of atomization for a diatomic molecule is the same as its bond dissociation energy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard enthalpy of formation of water is -286 kJ/mol. The enthalpy change for the reaction 2H2O(l) → 2H2(g) + O2(g) is
questions.explanation: Reverse of formation reaction of 2 moles of water. ΔH = -2 * (-286) = +572 kJ.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The entropy of a perfectly crystalline substance at absolute zero is
questions.explanation: Third law of thermodynamics.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a spontaneous process, the Gibbs free energy change ΔG is
questions.explanation: For spontaneity at constant T and P, ΔG < 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The unit of entropy is
questions.explanation: Entropy is usually expressed in J K-1 mol-1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is true for an endothermic reaction that is spontaneous?
questions.explanation: For an endothermic (ΔH > 0) reaction to be spontaneous, ΔS must be positive and temperature high so that TΔS > ΔH.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If ΔG for a reaction is zero, the reaction is
questions.explanation: ΔG = 0 indicates equilibrium.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The heat of neutralization of a strong acid and a strong base is
questions.explanation: The standard heat of neutralization is -57.1 kJ/mol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Hess's law is useful for calculating

options.A: enthalpy changes that cannot be measured directly

questions.explanation: Hess's law allows the calculation of enthalpy changes for reactions that are difficult to carry out directly.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The enthalpy of vaporization of water is 40.7 kJ/mol. The entropy change for the vaporization of water at 100°C is
questions.explanation: ΔS = ΔHvap/T = 40700 J/mol / 373 K ≈ 109 J K-1 mol-1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For which of the following processes, ΔS is negative?
questions.explanation: Two atoms combing to form a molecule decreases randomness, so entropy decreases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The enthalpy change for the reaction C2H4(g) + H2(g) → C2H6(g) can be calculated from the bond energies of
questions.explanation: Need bond energies of reactants (C=C, C-H, H-H) and products (C-C, C-H).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The heat of combustion of methane is -890 kJ/mol. The heat evolved when 16 g of methane is burned completely is
questions.explanation: 16 g of methane is 1 mole, so heat evolved is 890 kJ.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard enthalpy of formation of NH3(g) is -46 kJ/mol. The enthalpy change for the reaction 2NH3(g) → N2(g) + 3H2(g) is
questions.explanation: Reverse of formation reaction of 2 moles of NH3. ΔH = -2 * (-46) = +92 kJ.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The entropy of the universe always

options.C: remains constant for a spontaneous process

questions.explanation: Second law of thermodynamics: total entropy of the universe always increases for a spontaneous process.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction to be non-spontaneous at all temperatures, which of the following must be true?
questions.explanation: If ΔH > 0 and ΔS < 0, then ΔG = ΔH - TΔS will always be positive, so reaction is non-spontaneous at all temperatures.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The latent heat of fusion of ice is 6.0 kJ/mol. The entropy change for the fusion of ice at 0°C is
questions.explanation: ΔS = ΔHfusion/T = 6000 J/mol / 273 K ≈ 22 J K-1 mol-1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following reactions, ΔS is positive?
questions.explanation: In (4), number of moles of gas remains same, so ΔS is positive due to increase in disorder? Actually, (4) has 2 moles reactant and 2 moles product, so ΔS is small but can be positive. (1), (2), (3) have decrease in number of gas moles, so ΔS is negative.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The Gibbs free energy change ΔG is related to ΔH and ΔS by the equation
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Some of the properties of the two species, NO3- and H3O+ are described below. Which one of them is correct?

options.A: Dissimilar in hybridization for the central atom with different structures
options.B: Isostructural with same hybridization for the central atom
options.C: Isostructural with different hybridization for the central atom
options.D: Similar in hybridization for the central atom with different structures

questions.explanation: NO3- is sp2 hybridized and trigonal planar, H3O+ is sp3 hybridized and pyramidal.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: What is the dominant intermolecular force or bond that must be overcome in converting liquid CH3OH to a gas?
questions.explanation: Methanol exhibits hydrogen bonding, which is the dominant intermolecular force to be overcome during vaporization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to MO theory which of the following lists ranks the nitrogen species in terms of increasing bond order?
questions.explanation: Bond orders: N22- (2), N2- (2.5), N2 (3). So increasing order: N22- < N2- < N2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following molecules/ions BF3, NO2-, NH2- and H2O the central atom is sp2 hybridized ?
questions.explanation: BF3 and NO2- have sp2 hybridization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the case of alkali metals, the covalent character decreases in the order
questions.explanation: For same cation larger anion more will be covalent character. I- is largest, F- is smallest. So MI > MBr > MCl > MF.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Four diatomic species are listed below the different sequences. Which of these presents the correct order of their increasing bond order?
questions.explanation: Bond orders: He2+ (0.5), O2- (1.5), NO (2.5), C22- (3). So increasing order: He2+ < O2- < NO < C22-.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The angular shape of molecule O3 consists of
questions.explanation: O3 has a bent shape with one O=O double bond (1 sigma, 1 pi) and one O-O single bond (1 sigma). Total 2 sigma and 1 pi bond? Actually, the pi bond is delocalized over both bonds, so the structure has 2 sigma bonds and a delocalized pi bond containg 2 electrons. Option (1) says 2 sigma and 1 pi, which is correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of increasing bond angles in the following triatomic species is
questions.explanation: Bond angles: NO2- (115°), NO2 (134°), NO2+ (180°). So increasing order: NO2- < NO2 < NO2+.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of (C-O) bond length among CO, CO32-, CO2 is
questions.explanation: Bond order: CO (3), CO2 (2), CO32- (1.33). Bond length is inversely proportional to bond order. So CO (shortest) < CO2 < CO32- (longest).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following pairs, the two species are iso-structural ?
questions.explanation: Both BrO3- and XeO3 have pyramidal geometry.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is not a correct statement?

options.A: The electron-deficient molecules can act as Lewis acids
options.B: The canonical structures have no real existence
options.C: Every AB5 molecule does in fact have square pyramid structure
options.D: Multiple bonds are always shorter than corresponding single bonds

questions.explanation: AB5 molecules can have trigonal bipyramidal or square pyramidal structures, not always square pyramid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of unpaired electrons in a paramagnetic diatomic molecule of an element with atomic number 16 is
questions.explanation: Atomic number 16 is sulfur (S2). MO configuration: KK σ₂ₛ2 σ₂ₛ2 σ₂ₚz2 π₂ₚₓ2 π₂ₚᵧ2 π₂ₚₓ1 π*₂ₚᵧ1. Two unpaired electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following species has a linear shape ?
questions.explanation: NO2+ is linear.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The electronegativity difference between N and F is greater than that between N and H yet the dipole moment of NH3 (1.5 D) is larger than that of NF3 (0.2 D). This is because

options.A: In NH3 as well as in NF3 the atomic dipole and bond dipole are in the same direction
options.B: In NH3 the atomic dipole and bond dipole are in the same direction whereas in NF3 these are in opposite directions
options.C: In NH3 as well as NF3 the atomic dipole and bond dipole are in opposite directions
options.D: In NH3 the atomic dipole and bond dipole are in the opposite directions whereas in NF3 these are in the same directions

questions.explanation: In NH3, the lone pair dipole and bond dipoles add up. In NF3, the lone pair dipole opposes the resultant bond dipole, resulting in a lower net dipole.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecules has trigonal planar geometry?
questions.explanation: BF3 is trigonal planar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following would have a permanent dipole moment?
questions.explanation: SF4 has see-saw shape and a permanent dipole moment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order in which the O - O bond length increases in the following is
questions.explanation: O-O bond order: O2 (2) < O3 (1.5) < H2O2 (1). Bond length inversely related, so O2 (shortest) < O3 < H2O2 (longest).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following oxides is expected to exhibit paramagnetic behaviour?
questions.explanation: ClO2 has an odd number of electrons (total 17+8=33), so it is paramagnetic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following species contains three bond pairs and two lone pairs around the central atom?
questions.explanation: ClF3 has 3 bond pairs and 2 lone pairs, T-shaped.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Bond order of 2.5 is shown by
questions.explanation: O2+ has bond order 2.5.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The outer orbitals of C in ethene molecule can be considered to be hybridized to give three equivalent sp2 orbitals. The total number of sigma (σ) and pi (π) bonds in ethene molecule is
questions.explanation: Ethene has 5 sigma bonds (4 C-H and 1 C-C) and 1 pi bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The geometry of electron pairs around I in IF5 is
questions.explanation: IF5: H = 1/2 = 12/2 = 6, sp3d2. Electron pair geometry is octahedral.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following pair both the species have sp3 hybridization?
questions.explanation: NF3 and H2O both have sp3 hybridization (though different shapes).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the hydrocarbon CH3 – C ≡ C – CH = CH2 The state of hybridization of carbons 1, 3 and 5 are in the following sequence
questions.explanation: C1 (CH3) is sp3, C3 (≡C) is sp, C5 (=CH2) is sp2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecule does not possess a permanent dipole moment?
questions.explanation: CS2 is linear, so μ = 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following compound there is more than one kind of hybridization (sp, sp2, sp3) for carbon?
questions.explanation: CH3-CH=CH-CH3 contains both sp3 (CH3) and sp2 (=CH-) hybridized carbons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following bonds has the highest energy?
questions.explanation: C≡C has three bonds and the highest bond energy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The structure and hybridization of Si(CH3)4 is
questions.explanation: Si is sp3 hybridized, tetrahedral.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of bonding electron pairs in N2 on the basis of molecular orbital theory is
questions.explanation: N2 = σ₁ₛ2, σ₁ₛ2, σ₂ₛ2, σ₂ₛ2, π₂ₚₓ2 = π₂ₚᵧ2, σ₂ₚz2. Bonding pairs: σ₁ₛ, σ₂ₛ, π₂ₚₓ, π₂ₚᵧ, σ₂ₚz. Total 5 bonding pairs.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which compound is electron deficient?
questions.explanation: BCl3 has only 6 electrons around B, so it is electron deficient.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which compound form polymer due to H-bond?
questions.explanation: HF forms strong intermolecular hydrogen bonds, leading to polymeric associations.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Cation and anion combines in a crystal to form following type of compound
questions.explanation: Cation and anion form ionic bond in crystal.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which compound has tetrahedral structure?
questions.explanation: XeO4 has sp3 hybridization and tetrahedral structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following bond angle is maximum?
questions.explanation: PCl6- is octahedral with 90° and 180° angles. The maximum bond angle is 180°.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In X - H...Y, X and Y both are electronegative elements. Then

options.A: Electron density on X will increase and on H will decrease
options.B: In both electron density will increase
options.C: In both electron density will decrease
options.D: On X electron density will increase and on H increases

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding withdraws electron density from H, increasing electron density on X.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Main axis of a diatomic molecule is z, molecular orbital px and px overlap to form which of the following orbitals?
questions.explanation: px-px overlap sideways forms a π molecular orbital.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The state of hybridization of C2, C3, C5 and C6 of the hydrocarbon, is in the following sequence
questions.explanation: Image based.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For two ionic solids CaO and KI, identify the wrong statement among the following.

options.B: Lattice energy of CaO is much larger than that of KI

questions.explanation: KI is ionic and soluble in water, not in non-polar benzene.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following organic compounds has same hybridization as its combustion product -(CO2) ?
questions.explanation: CO2 has sp hybridization. Ethyne (C2H2) also has sp hybridization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The formal charges of oxygen labelled 3,2,1 in ozone respectively are

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is not correct for sigma- and pi- bonds formed between two carbon atoms?

options.A: Sigma-bond is stronger than a pi-bond
options.B: Bond energies of sigma- and pi-bonds are of the order of 264 kJ/mol and 347 kJ/mol, respectively
options.C: Free rotation of atoms about a sigma-bond is allowed but not in case of a pi-bond
options.D: Sigma-bond determines the direction between carbon atoms but a pi-bond has no effect in this regard

questions.explanation: σ bond is stronger than π bond : B.E. of σ bond is more than π bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: H2O is polar, whereas BeF2 is not. It is because

options.A: The electronegativity of F is greater than that of O
options.B: H2O involves hydrogen bonding whereas BeF2 is a discrete molecule
options.C: H2O is linear and BeF2 is angular
options.D: H2O is angular and BeF2 is linear

questions.explanation: H2O have angular shape because of two lone pair. BeF2 have linear shape. H2O is polar, BeF2 is linear.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The table shown below gives the bond dissociation energies (Ediss) for single covalent bonds of carbon(C) atoms with element A, B, Z and D. Which element has the smallest atoms? Bond Ediss(kJ mol-1) C- A 240 C- B 328 C- Z 276 C- D 485
questions.explanation: More the dissociation energy i.e. stronger the (C- D) bond. D atoms have smaller size.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of C- O bond order among CO, CO32-, CO2 is
questions.explanation: Image based explanation. CO has triple bond (bond order 3), CO2 has double bonds (bond order 2), CO32- has resonance with bond order 1.33. So CO32- < CO2 < CO.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following species contains three bond pairs and one lone pair around the central atom?
questions.explanation: PCl3 have 3BP and 1LP

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following pairs of chemical species contains the ions having same hybridisation of the central atoms?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The geometry of electron pairs around S in SF6 is
questions.explanation: SF6 : H = 1/2 = 12/2 = 6 → sp3d2 → Octahedral geometry

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the hydrocarbon CH3CH = CH2CH = CH The state of hybridization of carbons 1, 3 and 5 are in the following sequence
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The BCl3 is a planar molecule whereas NCl3 is pyramidal because

options.A: Nitrogen atom is smaller than boron atom
options.B: BCl3 has no lone pair but NCl3 has a lone pair of electrons
options.C: B - Cl bond is more polar than N - Cl bond
options.D: N - Cl bond is more covalent than B - Cl bond

questions.explanation: BCl3 have planar structure, NCl3 have pyramidal structure due to lone pair.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which compound has planar structure?
questions.explanation: XeF4 have H = 1/2 = 12/2 = 6 = sp3d2 octahedral geometry but due to two lone pair have planar (square planar) shape.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following pairs are isostructural?
questions.explanation: XeF2: H = 1/2 = 10/2 = 5 → sp3d, linear. IF2-: I has 7 valence electrons, plus 1 from negative charge, minus 2 for bonds, leaves 6 electrons as 3 lone pairs. H = 1/2 = 10/2 = 5 → sp3d, linear.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In BrF3 molecule, the lone pairs occupy equatorial positions to minimize

options.C: Lone pair-lone pair repulsion and lone pair-bond pair repulsion

questions.explanation: lp- lp repulsion and lp- bp repulsion will be minimum when lp occupy equatorial positions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
